---
title: "Social Mixtures"
author: "Kelsey MacCuish"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r, load libraries}
library(haven)
library(dplyr)
library(tableone)
library(broom)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(mice)
library(corrplot)
library(msm)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(emmeans)
library(mitools) 
library(corrplot)
source("Functions_rmph.R")
library(lmtp)
library(mlr3extralearners)
library(xgboost)
library(lmtp)
library(sl3)
library(origami) 
library(future)
library(future.apply)
set.seed(124)
```


## Read in data
```{r, read data}
cham_all <- read_dta("../../../Code/torres_stressor_01j_nohsn.dta") # read in CHAMACOS data

head(cham_all) # first 6 rows of dataset
str(cham_all) # variables and variable types
dim(cham_all) # number of observations and variables
names(cham_all) # names of variables
```


## Define exposures, outcomes, covariates
```{r, define exposures, outcomes, covariates}
## Exposures: 
# Poverty
# Food insecurity
# Household crowding (PPBR) (PPR for sensitivity)
# Deportation worry
# Chronic stressors (personal health problems, health problem in someone else, job difficulties, financial, relationships, taking care of friend/relative w/ health condition, parenting, natural disasters, COVID-19, something else)


## Outcomes: 
# Primary:
# Cognitive performance:
# Memory
# Executive function
# Verbal fluency
# Global composition

## Covariates: 
# Age (baseline)
# Education (baseline)
# Nativity/age at migration (baseline)
# Marital status (18-year, MatCog)
# Employment status (MatCog)
# Language of interview (MatCog)
```


## Data cleaning
```{r, clean variables for analysis}
## Exposures: 

# Poverty status: povcat_mc1 and pov_binary
cham <- cham_all %>% mutate(povcat_mc1 = ifelse(povcat_mc1 == 1, "At or below poverty",
                                     ifelse(povcat_mc1 == 2, "200% FPL",
                                     ifelse(povcat_mc1 == 3, ">=200% FPL", "Missing"))))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(pov_binary = ifelse(povcat_mc1 == "At or below poverty", 
                                            "At or below poverty", 
                                     ifelse(povcat_mc1 %in% c("200% FPL", ">=200% FPL"), "Above poverty", NA)))

# Food insecurity: ficat_mc1
cham <- cham %>% mutate(ficat_mc1 = ifelse(ficat_mc1 == 1, "High security", 
                                    ifelse(ficat_mc1 == 2, "Low security", "Very low security")))

# Binary food insecurity: fi_bin_mc1
cham <- cham %>% mutate(fi_bin_mc1 = ifelse(ficat_mc1 == "High security", "Food secure", "Insecure"))

# Household crowding: ppb2_mc1 and ppr_1
# more than 2 people per bedroom: ppb2_mc1 
cham <- cham %>% mutate(ppb2_mc1 = ifelse(ppb2_mc1 == 0, "<=2", ">2"))

# more than 1 person per room: ppr_1 and ppr_1_update
cham <- cham %>% mutate(ppr_1 = ifelse(dens1_cat_mc1 == 1 | dens1_cat_mc1 == 2, "<=1", 
                                ifelse(dens1_cat_mc1 == 3 | dens1_cat_mc1 == 4, ">1", NA)))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(ppr_1_update = ifelse(dens2_cat_mc1 == 1 | dens2_cat_mc1 == 2, "<=1", 
                                ifelse(dens2_cat_mc1 == 3 | dens2_cat_mc1 == 4, ">1", NA)))

# Deportation worry: wry_dep_mc1
cham <- cham %>% mutate(wry_dep_mc1 = ifelse(wry_dep_mc1 == 1, "A lot", 
                                      ifelse(wry_dep_mc1 == 2, "Some", 
                                      ifelse(wry_dep_mc1 == 3, "Not much", "Not at all"))))

# Binary deportation worry: 
cham <- cham %>% mutate(wry_bin_mc1 = ifelse(wry_dep_mc1 %in% c("Not much", "Not at all"), "No worry", "Worry"))

# Chronic stress: personal health: stress_personal_health_mc1
#cham <- cham %>% mutate(stress_personal_health_mc1 = ifelse(stress1_mc1 == 0, "No", "Yes"))

# Chronic stress: health problem in someone close to you: stress_other_health_mc1
#cham <- cham %>% mutate(stress_other_health_mc1 = ifelse(stress2_mc1 == 0, "No", "Yes"))

# Chronic stress: job: stress_job_mc1
cham <- cham %>% mutate(stress_job_mc1 = ifelse(stress3_mc1 == 0, "No", "Yes"))

# Chronic stress: financial: stress_fin_mc1
#cham <- cham %>% mutate(stress_fin_mc1 = ifelse(stress4_mc1 == 0, "No", "Yes"))

# Chronic stress: relationship: stress_relat_mc1
#cham <- cham %>% mutate(stress_relat_mc1 = ifelse(stress5_mc1 == 0, "No", "Yes"))

# Chronic stress: taking care of someone with health condition: stress_take_care_mc1
#cham <- cham %>% mutate(stress_take_care_mc1 = ifelse(stress6_mc1 == 0, "No", "Yes"))

# Chronic stress: parenting: stress_parent_mc1
#cham <- cham %>% mutate(stress_parent_mc1 = ifelse(stress7_mc1 == 0, "No", "Yes"))

# Chronic stress: natural disaster: stress_disaster_mc1
#cham <- cham %>% mutate(stress_disaster_mc1 = ifelse(stress8_mc1 == 0, "No", "Yes"))

# Chronic stress: COVID-19: stress_covid_mc1
#cham <- cham %>% mutate(stress_covid_mc1 = ifelse(stress9_mc1 == 0, "No", "Yes"))

# Chronic stress: something else: stress_else_mc1
#cham <- cham %>% mutate(stress_else_mc1 = ifelse(stress10_mc1 == 0, "No", "Yes"))

# Chronic stress: count
cham <- cham %>% mutate(stress_count_mc1 = #stress1_mc1 + stress2_mc1 + 
                        stress3_mc1) #+ 
                                       #stress4_mc1 + stress5_mc1 + stress6_mc1 + 
                                       #stress7_mc1 + #stress8_mc1 + stress9_mc1 + 
                                       #stress10_mc1)

# Chronic stress: binary chronic stress
cham <- cham %>% mutate(stress_bin_mc1 = ifelse(stress_count_mc1 == 0, "No", "Yes"))

## Covariates: 

# Age at baseline: momdl_age
cham %>% dplyr::select(momdl_age)

# Parental education
# if don't know both parents education -> Don't know/Don't remember
# if don't know mom & dad never attended or don't know dad & mom never attended or neither attended -> None, never attended school
# all others -> any formal education
cham <- cham %>% mutate(parent_educ = 
                        ifelse(medu_mc1 == 9 &
                               fedu_mc1 == 9,
                               "Dont know/dont remember",
                        ifelse(medu_mc1 == 9 &
                               fedu_mc1 == 1
                               | medu_mc1 == 1 & 
                               fedu_mc1 == 9 | 
                               medu_mc1 == 1 & 
                               fedu_mc1 == 1, 
                               "No formal education",
                               "any formal educ")))
                        
# Education at baseline: educcat_mom
cham <- cham %>% mutate(educcat_mom = ifelse(educcat_mom == 1, "<=6th grade", 
                                      ifelse(educcat_mom == 2, "7-11th grade", 
                                      ifelse(educcat_mom == 3, ">= HS graduate", NA))))

# Nativity/age at migration at baseline: ageusa_cat and ageusa_bl and ageusa_18

# calculate age participant came back to the US at matcog visit 1
# yrsusa_matcog = yrsusa_dl + (date_matcog - dlvrydt)/365.25 
# yrsusa_matcog is years in the US at maternal cognition study visit 1
cham <- cham %>% mutate(age_came_back_to_us = age_qx_mc1 - yrsusa_matcog)

# compare ageusa_bl with calculated age got back to the US
cham %>% dplyr::select(age_qx_mc1, yrsusa_matcog, ageusa_bl, age_came_back_to_us) %>% filter(!is.na(age_qx_mc1))

# if ageusa_bl is -9, participant was born in the US
cham <- cham %>% mutate(ageusa_bl = ifelse(ageusa_bl == -9, 0, ageusa_bl))

# if ageusa_bl is < 0, set to 0
cham <- cham %>% mutate(ageusa_bl = ifelse(ageusa_bl < 0, 0, ageusa_bl))

# if ageusa_bl is missing, and age_qx_mc1 = yrsusa_matcog, replace ageusa_bl with age_came_back_to_us
# if age at survey = number of years lived in the US, person has lived in the US their whole life
# i.e., they did not leave and come back to the US
cham <- cham %>% mutate(ageusa_bl = ifelse(is.na(ageusa_bl) & 
                                           age_qx_mc1 - yrsusa_matcog < 1, age_came_back_to_us, 
                                                     ageusa_bl))

# check instances where there are discrepancies between ageusa_bl and calculated age_came_back_to_us
cham %>% filter(abs(ageusa_bl - age_came_back_to_us) > 2) %>% 
  dplyr::select(newid, age_qx_mc1, yrsusa_matcog, ageusa_bl, age_came_back_to_us, country_mom)

# check distribution of age came to the US variable
ggplot(data = cham, aes(x = ageusa_bl)) + 
  geom_histogram()

cham %>% filter(is.na(ageusa_bl)) %>% dplyr::select(newid, ageusa_bl, age_came_back_to_us,
                                                    country_mom, age_qx_mc1, yrsusa_matcog)

# set ageusa_bl to 0 for individuals with country_mom = 1 
cham <- cham %>% mutate(ageusa_bl = ifelse(is.na(ageusa_bl) & country_mom == 1, 0, ageusa_bl))


# categorize age came to the US variable 
cham <- cham %>% mutate(ageusa_cat = 
                        ifelse(country_mom == 1, "0",
                        ifelse(ageusa_bl > 0 & ageusa_bl < 18, "<1-17", 
                        ifelse(ageusa_bl >= 18 & ageusa_bl < 25, "18-24",
                        ifelse(ageusa_bl >= 25, "25+", NA)))))

# check age came to the us distribution among matcog participants 
cham %>% filter(!is.na(age_qx_mc1)) %>% group_by(ageusa_cat) %>% count()


# check those missing age that came to the US variable
cham %>% filter(is.na(ageusa_cat)) %>% dplyr::select(newid, country_mom, ageusa_bl, ageusa_cat,
                                                     age_came_back_to_us, newid, yrsusa_matcog, 
                                                     age_qx_mc1)

# 7 individuals with ageusa_bl = 0 and country_mom = 2
# recategorize these into "<1-17" group
cham <- cham %>% mutate(ageusa_cat = ifelse(country_mom == 2 & is.na(ageusa_cat), "<1-17", ageusa_cat))

# replace the two missing their ageusa_cat values with their calculated age_came_back_to_us values
cham <- cham %>% mutate(ageusa_cat = ifelse(is.na(ageusa_cat), age_came_back_to_us, ageusa_cat))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(ageusa_cat = ifelse(ageusa_cat == 17.3915138244629, "<1-17", 
                                     ifelse(ageusa_cat == 23.0636558532715, "18-24", 
                                            ageusa_cat)))

cham %>% group_by(ageusa_cat) %>% count()

# create adulthood arrival to the US variable: born in US, <18 and 18+
cham <- cham %>% mutate(ageusa_18 = ifelse(ageusa_cat == "0", "0",
                                    ifelse(ageusa_cat == "<1-17", "<18", "18+")))

# Marriage: marstat_m18y and married (matcog)
cham <- cham %>% mutate(marstat_m18y = ifelse(marstat_m18y == 1 | 
                                              marstat_m18y == 2, "married", "not married"))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(married = ifelse(married_mc1 == 1 | 
                                         married_mc1 == 2, "married", "not married"))

# Employment status: work_mc1 and workc_mc1
cham <- cham %>% mutate(work_mc1 = 
                        ifelse(work_mc1 == 1, "Worked since last visit",
                        ifelse(work_mc1 == 0, "Did not work since last visit", NA)))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(workc_mc1 = ifelse(workc_mc1 == 1, "Working now",
                                    ifelse(workc_mc1 == 0, "Not working now",
                                           NA)))

# Language of interview: lang_exam_mc1
cham <- cham %>% mutate(lang_exam_mc1 = ifelse(lang_exam_mc1 == 1, "English", "Spanish"))

# Depression: depresscat_mc1, depresscat_m18y, depresscat_m16y, depress_mc1, depress_m18y, depress_m16y and dep_mc1
cham <- cham %>% mutate(depresscat_mc1 = ifelse(depresscat_mc1 == 0, "Not depressed", "Depressed"))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(depresscat_m18y = ifelse(depresscat_m18y == 0, "Not depressed",
                                                 "Depressed"))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(depresscat_m16y = ifelse(depresscat_m16y == 0, "Not depressed",
                                                 "Depressed"))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(dep_mc1 = ifelse(dep_mc1 == 0, "No",
                                  ifelse(dep_mc1 == 1, "Yes", 
                                  ifelse(dep_mc1 == 9, "Don't know", NA))))

# Anxiety: gadscore_mc1, gad7_score_m18y, gad7_score_m16y, gad4cat_mc1, gad7_4cat_m18y, gad7_4cat_m16y, anx_mc1
cham <- cham %>% mutate(gad4cat_mc1 = ifelse(gad4cat_mc1 == 0, "Minimal", 
                                      ifelse(gad4cat_mc1 == 1, "Mild", 
                                      ifelse(gad4cat_mc1 == 2, "Moderate", "Severe"))))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(gad7_4cat_m18y = ifelse(gad7_4cat_m18y == 0, "Minimal", 
                                         ifelse(gad7_4cat_m18y == 1, "Mild", 
                                         ifelse(gad7_4cat_m18y == 2, "Moderate", "Severe"))))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(gad7_4cat_m16y = ifelse(gad7_4cat_m16y == 0, "Minimal", 
                                         ifelse(gad7_4cat_m16y == 1, "Mild", 
                                         ifelse(gad7_4cat_m16y == 2, "Moderate", "Severe"))))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(anx_mc1 = ifelse(anx_mc1 == 0, "No", 
                                  ifelse(anx_mc1 == 1, "Yes", "Don't know")))

cham %>% dplyr::select(gadscore_mc1, gad7_score_m18y, gad7_score_m16y, 
                       gad4cat_mc1, gad7_4cat_m18y, gad7_4cat_m16y, anx_mc1)

# Self-reported chronic health conditions: diab_mc1, hbp_mc1, heart_mc1, cancer_mc1, asth_mc1, thyr_mc1

# re-code don't knows to 999
cham <- cham %>% 
  mutate(diab_mc1 = ifelse(diab_mc1 == 9, 999, diab_mc1),
         hbp_mc1 = ifelse(hbp_mc1 == 9, 999, hbp_mc1),
         # chol_mc1 = ifelse(chol_mc1 == 9, 999, chol_mc1),
         heart_mc1 = ifelse(heart_mc1 == 9, 999, heart_mc1), 
         cancer_mc1 = ifelse(cancer_mc1 == 9, 999, cancer_mc1), 
         asth_mc1 = ifelse(asth_mc1 == 9, 999, asth_mc1), 
         thyr_mc1 = ifelse(thyr_mc1 == 9, 999, thyr_mc1)) 

# check distribution of each self-rated doctor diagnosed health condition
cham %>% group_by(diab_mc1) %>% count() # only 1 person missing diabetes score
cham %>% group_by(hbp_mc1) %>% count() # 3 missing HBP score
cham %>% group_by(chol_mc1) %>% count() # 6 missing cholesterol
cham %>% group_by(heart_mc1) %>% count() # 4 missing heart issues
cham %>% group_by(cancer_mc1) %>% count() # 4 missing cancer
cham %>% group_by(asth_mc1) %>% count() # 2 missing asthma
cham %>% group_by(thyr_mc1) %>% count() # 0 missing thyroid
cham %>% group_by(dep_mc1) %>% count() # 7 missing depression 
cham %>% group_by(anx_mc1) %>% count() # 9 missing anxiety


# For those who don't know high blood pressure: 
# Check bpcat_mc1 variable for blood pressure information
# Replace the don't knows with 1 if have high blood pressure, 0 if no HBP
# only don't have hypertension if 0 for hbp_mc1 and bpcat_mc1 is 1 or 2 
cham %>% filter(hbp_mc1 == 999)
cham %>% filter(hbp_mc1 == 0 & bpcat_mc1 %in% c(3, 4))

recat_bp <- cham %>% filter(hbp_mc1 == 0 & bpcat_mc1 %in% c(3, 4))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(hbp_mc1 = ifelse(newid %in% c(recat_bp$newid), 1, 
                                                         hbp_mc1))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(hbp_mc1 = ifelse(newid == 99 & bpcat_mc1 == 3, 1,
                                  ifelse(newid == 102 & bpcat_mc1 == 1, 0,
                                  ifelse(newid == 617 & bpcat_mc1 == 1, 0,
                                         hbp_mc1))))

# For those who don't know diabetes:
# Make sure they had fasting blood taken and check gluccat_mc1 variable for diabetes information
# Replace the don't knows with 1 if diabetes and 0 if no diabetes/pre-diabetes (if fasting)
# if not fasting, check hba1c score 

cham %>% filter(diab_mc1 == 999)

# check: among those who self report no diabetes, how many do not have a fasting blood
# sugar score and have a hba1ccat_mc1 score that show diabetes
cham %>% filter(diab_mc1 == 0 & fast_bld_mc1 == 0 & hba1ccat_mc1 == 4)

# check: among those who report no diabetes, how many do have a fasting blood sugar
# and blood sugar is in diabetes category
recat_diab <- cham %>% filter(diab_mc1 == 0 & fast_bld_mc1 == 1 & gluccat_mc1 == 3)

cham <- cham %>% mutate(diab_mc1 = ifelse(newid %in% c(recat_diab$newid), 1, 
                                          diab_mc1))

cham <- cham %>% mutate(diab_mc1 = ifelse(newid == 222 & gluccat_mc1 == 2, 0, diab_mc1))

cham %>% group_by(diab_mc1) %>% count()

cham %>% filter(diab_mc1 == 0 & is.na(fast_bld_mc1))

# 4 missing heart issues - can't adjust b/c other heart variables are from 18 year and show 
# no heart issues - can't be sure they didn't develop heart issues between then and now 


# For those who don't know cancer: 
# Check cancer_m18y variable
# Replace don't knows with 1 if ever had cancer 
cham %>% filter(cancer_mc1 == 999)

cham <- cham %>% mutate(cancer_mc1 = ifelse(newid == 607 & cancer_m18y == 1, 1, cancer_mc1))

cham %>% group_by(cancer_mc1) %>% count()
# 3 left over missing cancer info

# 2 missing asthma - can't adjust b/c other asthma vars from 16y follow-up 
# show no asthma - can't be sure they didn't get asthma between then and now


# calculate overall self-rated health score
cham <- cham %>% mutate(summary_health = diab_mc1 + hbp_mc1 +
                                          heart_mc1 + cancer_mc1 + 
                                          asth_mc1 + thyr_mc1)

# re-code overall self-rated doctor diagnosed health scores into categories
# 0 - no major health issues
# 1 - 1 health issue
# 2-5: 2+ health issues
# 999 - 1 don't know, rest no
# 1000 = 1 yes, 1 dont know, rest no
# 1001 = 2 yes, 1 dont know, rest no
# 1002 = 3 yes, 1 dont know, rest no
# 2000 = 2 yes, 2 don't know, rest no
# NA - missing

# Group by yeses:
# No major health issues: 0
# 1 health issue: 1, 1000
# 2-5 health issues: 2-7, 1001, 1002, 2000
# Missing: NA
cham <- cham %>% mutate(summary_health_cat_yes = 
                                 ifelse(summary_health == 0 | 
                                        summary_health == 999, "no health issues", 
                                 ifelse(summary_health == 1 |
                                        summary_health == 1000, "1 health issue", 
                                 ifelse(summary_health > 1 & summary_health <= 7| 
                                        summary_health %in% c(1001, 1002, 2000), "2+",
                                        NA))))

# filter out woman missing most baseline data
cham <- cham %>% filter(!newid == 314)

# select relevant variables for regression models
cham_social <- cham %>% dplyr::select(newid, cham, qx_matcog,
                                      povcat_mc1, pov_binary,
                                      ficat_mc1, fi_bin_mc1, ppb2_mc1, ppr_1, ppr_1_update,
                                      wry_dep_mc1, wry_bin_mc1, 
                                      #stress_personal_health_mc1,
                                      #stress_other_health_mc1, 
                                      stress_job_mc1, 
                                      #stress_fin_mc1, 
                                      #stress_relat_mc1, stress_take_care_mc1, stress_parent_mc1, 
                                      #stress_disaster_mc1, stress_covid_mc1, 
                                      #stress_else_mc1, 
                                      stress_count_mc1, stress_bin_mc1, # exposures
                                      memory_mc1, execfun_rc_mc1, verbal_rc_mc1,
                                      global_rc_mc1, execfun_tbex_mc1,
                                      global_tbex_mc1,
                                      tmtb_fail_mc1, # outcomes
                                      age_qx_mc1, momdl_age,
                                      educcat_mom, parent_educ,
                                      ageusa_bl, ageusa_cat, ageusa_18,
                                      married, marstat_m18y,
                                      work_mc1, workc_mc1, lang_exam_mc1,
                                      depress_mc1, depress_m18y, depress_m16y,
                                      depresscat_mc1, depresscat_m18y, depresscat_m16y,
                                      dep_mc1,
                                      gadscore_mc1, gad7_score_m18y, gad7_score_m16y, 
                                      gad4cat_mc1, gad7_4cat_m18y, gad7_4cat_m16y, anx_mc1,
                                      summary_health, summary_health_cat_yes, 
                                      diab_mc1, hbp_mc1, heart_mc1, cancer_mc1,
                                      asth_mc1, thyr_mc1, # covariates
                                      hbp_bl, hbpage_bl, diab_bl, diabage_bl,
                                      cancer_bl, cancerage_bl,
                                      marstat_bl, worksp_bl, workc_bl, 
                                      povcat_bl, ipovcat_bl, ipovcat_bl_met) # baseline vars
                                      

# MatCog participants
cham_mc <- cham_social %>% filter(!is.na(age_qx_mc1))

# Check number of missing for each outcome, covariate, and exposure (Supplemental Appendix Table 1)
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(memory_mc1))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(execfun_rc_mc1))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(verbal_rc_mc1))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(global_rc_mc1))

cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(age_qx_mc1))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(ageusa_cat))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(married))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(educcat_mom))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(parent_educ))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(work_mc1))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(lang_exam_mc1))

cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(stress_bin_mc1))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(wry_bin_mc1))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(ppb2_mc1))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(fi_bin_mc1))
cham_mc %>% filter(is.na(pov_binary))

# Check how many people are missing each chronic stressor
#cham_mc %>% group_by(stress_personal_health_mc1) %>% count() # 172
#cham_mc %>% group_by(stress_other_health_mc1) %>% count() # 193
cham_mc %>% group_by(stress_job_mc1) %>% count() # 106
#cham_mc %>% group_by(stress_fin_mc1) %>% count() # 215
#cham_mc %>% group_by(stress_relat_mc1) %>% count() # 134
#cham_mc %>% group_by(stress_take_care_mc1) %>% count() # 93
#cham_mc %>% group_by(stress_parent_mc1) %>% count() # 124
#cham_mc %>% group_by(stress_else_mc1) %>% count()

cham_mc %>% group_by(educcat_mom) %>% count()
```


```{r, check correlation between chronic stress variables}
# 1 = personal health
# 2 = health prob in someone close
# 3 = job difficulties
# 4 = financial
# 5 = relationship
# 6 = care taking for someone with health condition
# 7 = parenting
# 8 = natural disasters
# 9 = COVID-19
# 10 = something else

cham_chron <- cham %>% mutate(pov_binary = ifelse(povcat_mc1 == "At or below poverty", 
                                            "At or below poverty", 
                                     ifelse(povcat_mc1 %in% c("200% FPL", ">=200% FPL"), "Above poverty", NA)))


cham_chron <- cham_chron %>% select(#stress1_mc1, stress2_mc1, 
                              stress3_mc1, #stress4_mc1, stress5_mc1, 
                              #stress6_mc1, stress7_mc1, stress8_mc1, stress9_mc1, stress10_mc1,
                              age_qx_mc1,
                              povcat_mc1)

cham_chron <- cham_chron %>% filter(!is.na(age_qx_mc1))

cham_chron <- cham_chron %>% select(-age_qx_mc1)

cham_chron <- cham_chron %>% mutate(#stress1_mc1 = as.numeric(stress1_mc1),
                                    #stress2_mc1 = as.numeric(stress2_mc1),
                                    stress3_mc1 = as.numeric(stress3_mc1),
                                    #stress4_mc1 = as.numeric(stress4_mc1),
                                    #stress5_mc1 = as.numeric(stress5_mc1),
                                    #stress6_mc1 = as.numeric(stress6_mc1),
                                    #stress7_mc1 = as.numeric(stress7_mc1),
                                    #stress8_mc1 = as.numeric(stress8_mc1),
                                    #stress9_mc1 = as.numeric(stress9_mc1),
                                    #stress10_mc1 = as.numeric(stress10_mc1),
                                    pov_binary = ifelse(povcat_mc1 == "At or below poverty" | is.na(povcat_mc1), 1, 0))

cham_chron <- cham_chron %>% select(-povcat_mc1)

#cham_chron_no_cov <- cham_chron %>% select(-stress9_mc1, -stress8_mc1)

cham_chron <- cham_chron %>% na.omit()

#cham_chron_no_cov <- cham_chron_no_cov %>% na.omit()

# Create correlation matrix
cor_chron <- cor(cham_chron)

colnames(cor_chron) <- c(#"personal health", "health prob someone close", 
                        "job", #"financial", "relationship", "care taking", "parenting",
                         #"natural disasters", "COVID-19", "something else", 
                        "poverty")

rownames(cor_chron) <- c(#"personal health", "health prob someone close", 
                        "job", #"financial", "relationship", "care taking", "parenting",
                        #"natural disasters", "COVID-19", "something else", 
                        "poverty")

# Create correlogram
corrplot(cor_chron, tl.col = "black")



### Removing COVID-19 and natural disaster chronic stressors
#cor_no_cov <- cor(cham_chron_no_cov)


#colnames(cor_no_cov) <- c("personal health", "health prob someone close", "job", "financial", "relationship", "care taking", "parenting",
                          #"something else", "poverty")

#rownames(cor_no_cov) <- c("personal health", "health prob someone close", "job", "financial", "relationship", "care taking", "parenting",
                          #"something else", "poverty")

# Create correlogram
#corrplot(cor_no_cov, tl.col = "black")
```


```{r, conduct multiple imputations}
# Define exposure variables
stressor_measures <- exposures <- c("stress_bin_mc1", "wry_bin_mc1", "ppb2_mc1", 
                                    "fi_bin_mc1", "pov_binary")

# Make exposure variables numeric
cham_propen <- cham_mc %>% mutate(stress_bin_mc1 = ifelse(stress_bin_mc1 == "No", 0, 1),
                              wry_bin_mc1 = ifelse(wry_bin_mc1 == "No worry", 0, 1),
                              ppb2_mc1 = ifelse(ppb2_mc1 == "<=2", 0, 1),
                              fi_bin_mc1 = ifelse(fi_bin_mc1 == "Food secure", 0, 1),
                              pov_binary = ifelse(pov_binary == "Above poverty", 0, 1))

# Create subset of data that includes variables of interest
cham_propen <- cham_propen %>% dplyr::select(age_qx_mc1, ageusa_18, married, educcat_mom,
                                  work_mc1, lang_exam_mc1,
                                  stress_bin_mc1, wry_bin_mc1, ppb2_mc1, fi_bin_mc1, pov_binary,
                                  memory_mc1, execfun_rc_mc1, verbal_rc_mc1, global_rc_mc1)

cham_propen <- as.data.frame(cham_propen)
cham_propen <- cham_propen %>% mutate(across(where(is.labelled), ~ as.factor(.)))
imp <- mice(cham_propen, m = 30)

# check that all missing values for covariates and exposures now have imputations
imp$imp
imp$method
```


```{r, table 1: descriptive statistics}
imputed_exposures <- c("ppb2_mc1", "fi_bin_mc1", "pov_binary")

# Calculate number of individuals where variable = 1 within each imputation
counts <- sapply(imputed_exposures, function(var) {
  sapply(1:30, function(i) {
    df <- complete(imp, i)
    sum(df[[var]] == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  })
})


# Make a dataframe of the number of individuals where variable = 1 for each imputed variable and for each of the 30 imputations
counts <- as.data.frame(counts)
counts$imputation <- 1:30
counts <- counts %>% relocate(imputation)

# Calculate averages across 30 imputations 
sum(counts$ppb2_mc1)/30
sum(counts$fi_bin_mc1)/30
sum(counts$pov_binary)/30



# Calculating proportions using Functions_rmph package
rbind(mi.n.p(imp, "stress_bin_mc1"))
rbind(mi.n.p(imp, "ppb2_mc1"))
rbind(mi.n.p(imp, "fi_bin_mc1"))
rbind(mi.n.p(imp, "wry_bin_mc1"))
rbind(mi.n.p(imp, "pov_binary"))


## Calculate average of continuous outcome within AND across each of the 30 imputations

# Calculate average memory score within each dataset (should be the same for all datasets bc no values were missing)
mean_mem <- with(imp, lm(memory_mc1~1))

# Pool across datasets using Rubin's rule
pooled_mem <- pool(mean_mem)
summary(pooled_mem)

# Calculating pooled mean, SE, SD manually
imp.cham <- complete(imp, action = "long")
MEAN <- tapply(imp.cham$memory_mc1, imp.cham$.imp, mean)
VAR.MEAN <- tapply(imp.cham$memory_mc1, 
                   imp.cham$.imp,
                   var) / nrow(imp$data)


SD <- tapply(imp.cham$memory_mc1,
             imp.cham$.imp,
             sd)

cbind("MEAN" = MEAN,
      "VAR.MEAN" = VAR.MEAN,
      "SD" = SD)

POOLED <- pool.scalar(MEAN,
                      VAR.MEAN,
                      nrow(imp$data))

data.frame(pooled.mean = POOLED$qbar,
           mean.var = POOLED$ubar,
           btw.var = POOLED$b,
           pooled.var = POOLED$t)


# get pooled SE of the mean from pooled variance of the mean
sqrt(POOLED$t)

# SD: average within-imputation SD over imputations
sum(SD)/30
rbind(mi.mean.se.sd(imp, "memory_mc1"))
sqrt((POOLED$ubar)*519)


# Calculate average executive function score within each dataset
mean_exec <- with(imp, lm(execfun_rc_mc1~1))
pooled_exec <- pool(mean_exec)
summary(pooled_exec)

# SD
sqrt(pooled_exec$pooled$ubar*519)
rbind(mi.mean.se.sd(imp, "execfun_rc_mc1"))


# Calculate average verbal fluency score within each dataset (should be the same for all datasets bc no values were missing)
mean_verb <- with(imp, lm(verbal_rc_mc1~1))
pooled_verb <- pool(mean_verb)
summary(pooled_verb)

# SD
sqrt(pooled_verb$pooled$ubar*519)
rbind(mi.mean.se.sd(imp, "verbal_rc_mc1"))


# Calculate average global function score within each dataset
mean_global <- with(imp, lm(global_rc_mc1~1))
pooled_global <- pool(mean_global)
summary(pooled_global)

# SD
sqrt(pooled_global$pooled$ubar*519)
rbind(mi.mean.se.sd(imp, "global_rc_mc1"))


# Calculate descriptive statistics for variables without missing values
cham_propen %>% summarize(mean = mean(age_qx_mc1),
                          sd = sd(age_qx_mc1))

cham_propen %>% group_by(ageusa_18) %>% count()

cham_propen %>% group_by(lang_exam_mc1) %>% count()

cham_propen %>% group_by(educcat_mom) %>% count()

cham_propen %>% group_by(married) %>% count()

cham_propen %>% group_by(work_mc1) %>% count()


# Calculate totals for exposures with missing values to compare to imputed datasets
cham_propen %>% group_by(pov_binary) %>% count()
cham_propen %>% group_by(fi_bin_mc1) %>% count()
cham_propen %>% group_by(ppb2_mc1) %>% count()
cham_propen %>% group_by(stress_bin_mc1) %>% count()
```


```{r, supplemental table 2: descriptive statistics of participant characteristics by individual exposure status}
imputed_exposures <- c("stress_bin_mc1", "ppb2_mc1", "fi_bin_mc1", "pov_binary", "wry_bin_mc1")

### Continuous variables

## Exposure: Chronic stressors

# Memory
imp$data$stress_bin_mc1 <- factor(imp$data$stress_bin_mc1)
fit <- with(imp, lm(memory_mc1 ~ stress_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ stress_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "memory_mc1", BY = "stress_bin_mc1"))


# Executive function
fit <- with(imp, lm(execfun_rc_mc1 ~ stress_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ stress_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

# Note: you can calculate the SD by taking the variance of the mean multiplied by the sample size of the subset of the variable and taking the square root of that product
# sqrt(var.mean*n_i)
rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "execfun_rc_mc1", BY = "stress_bin_mc1"))


# Verbal fluency
fit <- with(imp, lm(verbal_rc_mc1 ~ stress_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ stress_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "verbal_rc_mc1", BY = "stress_bin_mc1"))


# Global function
fit <- with(imp, lm(global_rc_mc1 ~ stress_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ stress_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "global_rc_mc1", BY = "stress_bin_mc1"))


# Age
fit <- with(imp, lm(age_qx_mc1 ~ stress_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ stress_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "age_qx_mc1", BY = "stress_bin_mc1"))



## Exposure: People per bedroom

# Memory
imp$data$ppb2_mc1 <- factor(imp$data$ppb2_mc1)
fit <- with(imp, lm(memory_mc1 ~ ppb2_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ ppb2_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "memory_mc1", BY = "ppb2_mc1"))


# Executive function
fit <- with(imp, lm(execfun_rc_mc1 ~ ppb2_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ ppb2_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "execfun_rc_mc1", BY = "ppb2_mc1"))


# Verbal fluency
fit <- with(imp, lm(verbal_rc_mc1 ~ ppb2_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ ppb2_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "verbal_rc_mc1", BY = "ppb2_mc1"))


# Global function
fit <- with(imp, lm(global_rc_mc1 ~ ppb2_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ ppb2_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "global_rc_mc1", BY = "ppb2_mc1"))


# Age
fit <- with(imp, lm(age_qx_mc1 ~ ppb2_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ ppb2_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "age_qx_mc1", BY = "ppb2_mc1"))



## Exposure: Food insecurity

# Memory
imp$data$fi_bin_mc1 <- factor(imp$data$fi_bin_mc1)
fit <- with(imp, lm(memory_mc1 ~ fi_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ fi_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "memory_mc1", BY = "fi_bin_mc1"))


# Executive function
fit <- with(imp, lm(execfun_rc_mc1 ~ fi_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ fi_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "execfun_rc_mc1", BY = "fi_bin_mc1"))


# Verbal fluency
fit <- with(imp, lm(verbal_rc_mc1 ~ fi_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ fi_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "verbal_rc_mc1", BY = "fi_bin_mc1"))


# Global function
fit <- with(imp, lm(global_rc_mc1 ~ fi_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ fi_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "global_rc_mc1", BY = "fi_bin_mc1"))


# Age
fit <- with(imp, lm(age_qx_mc1 ~ fi_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ fi_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "age_qx_mc1", BY = "fi_bin_mc1"))



## Exposure: Poverty

# Memory
imp$data$pov_binary <- factor(imp$data$pov_binary)
fit <- with(imp, lm(memory_mc1 ~ pov_binary))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ pov_binary)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "memory_mc1", BY = "pov_binary"))


# Executive function
fit <- with(imp, lm(execfun_rc_mc1 ~ pov_binary))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ pov_binary)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "execfun_rc_mc1", BY = "pov_binary"))


# Verbal fluency
fit <- with(imp, lm(verbal_rc_mc1 ~ pov_binary))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ pov_binary)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "verbal_rc_mc1", BY = "pov_binary"))


# Global function
fit <- with(imp, lm(global_rc_mc1 ~ pov_binary))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ pov_binary)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "global_rc_mc1", BY = "pov_binary"))


# Age
fit <- with(imp, lm(age_qx_mc1 ~ pov_binary))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ pov_binary)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "age_qx_mc1", BY = "pov_binary"))



## Exposure: Deportation worry

# Memory
imp$data$wry_bin_mc1 <- factor(imp$data$wry_bin_mc1)
fit <- with(imp, lm(memory_mc1 ~ wry_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ wry_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "memory_mc1", BY = "wry_bin_mc1"))


# Executive function
fit <- with(imp, lm(execfun_rc_mc1 ~ wry_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ wry_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "execfun_rc_mc1", BY = "wry_bin_mc1"))


# Verbal fluency
fit <- with(imp, lm(verbal_rc_mc1 ~ wry_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ wry_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "verbal_rc_mc1", BY = "wry_bin_mc1"))


# Global function
fit <- with(imp, lm(global_rc_mc1 ~ wry_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ wry_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "global_rc_mc1", BY = "wry_bin_mc1"))


# Age
fit <- with(imp, lm(age_qx_mc1 ~ wry_bin_mc1))
emm <- emmeans(fit, ~ wry_bin_mc1)
summary(emm)

rbind(mi.mean.se.sd.by(imp, "age_qx_mc1", BY = "wry_bin_mc1"))





### Categorical variables

## Nativity
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "stress_bin_mc1", BY = "ageusa_18"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "ppb2_mc1", BY = "ageusa_18"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "fi_bin_mc1", BY = "ageusa_18"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "pov_binary", BY = "ageusa_18"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "wry_bin_mc1", BY = "ageusa_18"))

strat_var <- "ageusa_18"

# Create summaries of counts of each exposure by stratification variable for all 30 imputations
summary_list <- lapply(1:30, function(i) {
  df <- complete(imp, i)

  df %>%
    group_by(.data[[strat_var]]) %>%
    summarise(across(all_of(imputed_exposures), ~sum(.x == 1), .names = "1s_counts_of_{.col}"),
              across(all_of(imputed_exposures), ~sum(.x == 0), .names = "0s_counts_of_{.col}")) %>%
    mutate(imputation = i)
})

# Create a single dataset with all counts of each exposure by stratification variable for all 30 imputations
all_counts <- bind_rows(summary_list)

# Average exposures stratified by covariate across imputations (round to nearest integer)
average_summary <- all_counts %>%
  group_by(.data[[strat_var]]) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("1s"), ~round(mean(.x))),
            across(starts_with("0s"), ~round(mean(.x))))

# Add column totals
totals <- average_summary %>%
  summarise(
    across(starts_with("1s"), sum, .names = "total_{.col}"),
    across(starts_with("0s"), sum, .names = "total_{.col}")
  )

# Add column totals to summary
average_summary_with_totals <- average_summary %>%
  bind_cols(totals[rep(1, nrow(average_summary)), ])

# Calculate proportion of totals
one_cols <- names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "^1s_")]
zero_cols <- names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "^0s_")]

for (col in one_cols) {
  total_col <- paste0("total_", col)
  prop_col <- paste0("prop_", col)
  average_summary_with_totals[[prop_col]] <- average_summary_with_totals[[col]] / average_summary_with_totals[[total_col]]
}

for (col in zero_cols) {
  total_col <- paste0("total_", col)
  prop_col <- paste0("prop_", col)
  average_summary_with_totals[[prop_col]] <- average_summary_with_totals[[col]] / average_summary_with_totals[[total_col]]
}

average_summary_with_props <- average_summary_with_totals %>% dplyr::select(-names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "total_")])
average_summary_with_props



## Language of assessment
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "stress_bin_mc1", BY = "lang_exam_mc1"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "ppb2_mc1", BY = "lang_exam_mc1"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "fi_bin_mc1", BY = "lang_exam_mc1"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "pov_binary", BY = "lang_exam_mc1"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "wry_bin_mc1", BY = "lang_exam_mc1"))

strat_var <- "lang_exam_mc1"

# Create summaries of counts of each exposure by stratification variable for all 30 imputations
summary_list <- lapply(1:30, function(i) {
  df <- complete(imp, i)

  df %>%
    group_by(.data[[strat_var]]) %>%
    summarise(across(all_of(imputed_exposures), ~sum(.x == 1), .names = "1s_counts_of_{.col}"),
              across(all_of(imputed_exposures), ~sum(.x == 0), .names = "0s_counts_of_{.col}")) %>%
    mutate(imputation = i)
})

# Create a single dataset with all counts of each exposure by stratification variable for all 30 imputations
all_counts <- bind_rows(summary_list)

# Average exposures stratified by covariate across imputations (round to nearest integer)
average_summary <- all_counts %>%
  group_by(.data[[strat_var]]) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("1s"), ~round(mean(.x))),
            across(starts_with("0s"), ~round(mean(.x))))

# Add column totals
totals <- average_summary %>%
  summarise(
    across(starts_with("1s"), sum, .names = "total_{.col}"),
    across(starts_with("0s"), sum, .names = "total_{.col}")
  )

# Add column totals to summary
average_summary_with_totals <- average_summary %>%
  bind_cols(totals[rep(1, nrow(average_summary)), ])

# Calculate proportion of totals
one_cols <- names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "^1s_")]
zero_cols <- names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "^0s_")]

for (col in one_cols) {
  total_col <- paste0("total_", col)
  prop_col <- paste0("prop_", col)
  average_summary_with_totals[[prop_col]] <- average_summary_with_totals[[col]] / average_summary_with_totals[[total_col]]
}

for (col in zero_cols) {
  total_col <- paste0("total_", col)
  prop_col <- paste0("prop_", col)
  average_summary_with_totals[[prop_col]] <- average_summary_with_totals[[col]] / average_summary_with_totals[[total_col]]
}

average_summary_with_props <- average_summary_with_totals %>% dplyr::select(-names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "total_")])
average_summary_with_props



## Educational attainment
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "stress_bin_mc1", BY = "educcat_mom"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "ppb2_mc1", BY = "educcat_mom"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "fi_bin_mc1", BY = "educcat_mom"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "pov_binary", BY = "educcat_mom"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "wry_bin_mc1", BY = "educcat_mom"))

strat_var <- "educcat_mom"

# Create summaries of counts of each exposure by stratification variable for all 30 imputations
summary_list <- lapply(1:30, function(i) {
  df <- complete(imp, i)

  df %>%
    group_by(.data[[strat_var]]) %>%
    summarise(across(all_of(imputed_exposures), ~sum(.x == 1), .names = "1s_counts_of_{.col}"),
              across(all_of(imputed_exposures), ~sum(.x == 0), .names = "0s_counts_of_{.col}")) %>%
    mutate(imputation = i)
})

# Create a single dataset with all counts of each exposure by stratification variable for all 30 imputations
all_counts <- bind_rows(summary_list)

# Average exposures stratified by covariate across imputations (round to nearest integer)
average_summary <- all_counts %>%
  group_by(.data[[strat_var]]) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("1s"), ~round(mean(.x))),
            across(starts_with("0s"), ~round(mean(.x))))

# Add column totals
totals <- average_summary %>%
  summarise(
    across(starts_with("1s"), sum, .names = "total_{.col}"),
    across(starts_with("0s"), sum, .names = "total_{.col}")
  )

# Add column totals to summary
average_summary_with_totals <- average_summary %>%
  bind_cols(totals[rep(1, nrow(average_summary)), ])

# Calculate proportion of totals
one_cols <- names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "^1s_")]
zero_cols <- names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "^0s_")]

for (col in one_cols) {
  total_col <- paste0("total_", col)
  prop_col <- paste0("prop_", col)
  average_summary_with_totals[[prop_col]] <- average_summary_with_totals[[col]] / average_summary_with_totals[[total_col]]
}

for (col in zero_cols) {
  total_col <- paste0("total_", col)
  prop_col <- paste0("prop_", col)
  average_summary_with_totals[[prop_col]] <- average_summary_with_totals[[col]] / average_summary_with_totals[[total_col]]
}

average_summary_with_props <- average_summary_with_totals %>% dplyr::select(-names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "total_")])
average_summary_with_props



## Marital status
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "stress_bin_mc1", BY = "married"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "ppb2_mc1", BY = "married"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "fi_bin_mc1", BY = "married"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "pov_binary", BY = "married"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "wry_bin_mc1", BY = "married"))

strat_var <- "married"

# Create summaries of counts of each exposure by stratification variable for all 30 imputations
summary_list <- lapply(1:30, function(i) {
  df <- complete(imp, i)

  df %>%
    group_by(.data[[strat_var]]) %>%
    summarise(across(all_of(imputed_exposures), ~sum(.x == 1), .names = "1s_counts_of_{.col}"),
              across(all_of(imputed_exposures), ~sum(.x == 0), .names = "0s_counts_of_{.col}")) %>%
    mutate(imputation = i)
})

# Create a single dataset with all counts of each exposure by stratification variable for all 30 imputations
all_counts <- bind_rows(summary_list)

# Average exposures stratified by covariate across imputations (round to nearest integer)
average_summary <- all_counts %>%
  group_by(.data[[strat_var]]) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("1s"), ~round(mean(.x))),
            across(starts_with("0s"), ~round(mean(.x))))

# Add column totals
totals <- average_summary %>%
  summarise(
    across(starts_with("1s"), sum, .names = "total_{.col}"),
    across(starts_with("0s"), sum, .names = "total_{.col}")
  )

# Add column totals to summary
average_summary_with_totals <- average_summary %>%
  bind_cols(totals[rep(1, nrow(average_summary)), ])

# Calculate proportion of totals
one_cols <- names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "^1s_")]
zero_cols <- names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "^0s_")]

for (col in one_cols) {
  total_col <- paste0("total_", col)
  prop_col <- paste0("prop_", col)
  average_summary_with_totals[[prop_col]] <- average_summary_with_totals[[col]] / average_summary_with_totals[[total_col]]
}

for (col in zero_cols) {
  total_col <- paste0("total_", col)
  prop_col <- paste0("prop_", col)
  average_summary_with_totals[[prop_col]] <- average_summary_with_totals[[col]] / average_summary_with_totals[[total_col]]
}

average_summary_with_props <- average_summary_with_totals %>% dplyr::select(-names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "total_")])
average_summary_with_props



## Employment status
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "stress_bin_mc1", BY = "work_mc1"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "ppb2_mc1", BY = "work_mc1"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "fi_bin_mc1", BY = "work_mc1"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "pov_binary", BY = "work_mc1"))
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "wry_bin_mc1", BY = "work_mc1"))

strat_var <- "work_mc1"

# Create summaries of counts of each exposure by stratification variable for all 30 imputations
summary_list <- lapply(1:30, function(i) {
  df <- complete(imp, i)

  df %>%
    group_by(.data[[strat_var]]) %>%
    summarise(across(all_of(imputed_exposures), ~sum(.x == 1), .names = "1s_counts_of_{.col}"),
              across(all_of(imputed_exposures), ~sum(.x == 0), .names = "0s_counts_of_{.col}")) %>%
    mutate(imputation = i)
})

# Create a single dataset with all counts of each exposure by stratification variable for all 30 imputations
all_counts <- bind_rows(summary_list)

# Average exposures stratified by covariate across imputations (round to nearest integer)
average_summary <- all_counts %>%
  group_by(.data[[strat_var]]) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("1s"), ~round(mean(.x))),
            across(starts_with("0s"), ~round(mean(.x))))

# Add column totals
totals <- average_summary %>%
  summarise(
    across(starts_with("1s"), sum, .names = "total_{.col}"),
    across(starts_with("0s"), sum, .names = "total_{.col}")
  )

# Add column totals to summary
average_summary_with_totals <- average_summary %>%
  bind_cols(totals[rep(1, nrow(average_summary)), ])

# Calculate proportion of totals
one_cols <- names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "^1s_")]
zero_cols <- names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "^0s_")]

for (col in one_cols) {
  total_col <- paste0("total_", col)
  prop_col <- paste0("prop_", col)
  average_summary_with_totals[[prop_col]] <- average_summary_with_totals[[col]] / average_summary_with_totals[[total_col]]
}

for (col in zero_cols) {
  total_col <- paste0("total_", col)
  prop_col <- paste0("prop_", col)
  average_summary_with_totals[[prop_col]] <- average_summary_with_totals[[col]] / average_summary_with_totals[[total_col]]
}

average_summary_with_props <- average_summary_with_totals %>% dplyr::select(-names(average_summary_with_totals)[str_detect(names(average_summary_with_totals), "total_")])
average_summary_with_props
```


```{r, supplemental table 3: number of social stressors experienced and most common combination of hardships for each number experienced}
# Get data from first imputation
first_imp <- complete(imp, 1)

# Make variables numeric
first_imp <- first_imp %>% mutate(stress_bin_mc1 = as.numeric(stress_bin_mc1),
                                  wry_bin_mc1 = as.numeric(wry_bin_mc1),
                                  ppb2_mc1 = as.numeric(ppb2_mc1),
                                  fi_bin_mc1 = as.numeric(fi_bin_mc1),
                                  pov_binary = as.numeric(pov_binary),
                                  stress_bin_mc1 = ifelse(stress_bin_mc1 == 2, 1, 0),
                                  wry_bin_mc1 = ifelse(wry_bin_mc1 == 2, 1, 0),
                                  ppb2_mc1 = ifelse(ppb2_mc1 == 2, 1, 0),
                                  fi_bin_mc1 = ifelse(fi_bin_mc1 == 2, 1, 0),
                                  pov_binary = ifelse(pov_binary == 2, 1, 0))


# Find the most common social stressors observed from first imputation

# Those who experienced no social stressors
first_imp %>% group_by(fi_bin_mc1, ppb2_mc1, wry_bin_mc1, stress_bin_mc1, pov_binary) %>% count() # 26


# Most common individual social stressor experienced
first_imp %>% group_by(stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 106
first_imp %>% group_by(wry_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 368
first_imp %>% group_by(ppb2_mc1) %>% count() # 110
first_imp %>% group_by(fi_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 208
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary) %>% count() # 192


# Most common pair of social stressors experienced together
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, fi_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 111
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, ppb2_mc1) %>% count() # 55
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, wry_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 147
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 53
first_imp %>% group_by(fi_bin_mc1, ppb2_mc1) %>% count() # 51
first_imp %>% group_by(fi_bin_mc1, wry_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 168
first_imp %>% group_by(fi_bin_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 63
first_imp %>% group_by(ppb2_mc1, wry_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 84
first_imp %>% group_by(ppb2_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 21
first_imp %>% group_by(wry_bin_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 84


# 3 most common social stressors experienced together
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, fi_bin_mc1, ppb2_mc1) %>% count() # 35
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, fi_bin_mc1, wry_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 90
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, fi_bin_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 42
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, ppb2_mc1, wry_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 44
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, ppb2_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 12
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, wry_bin_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 47
first_imp %>% group_by(fi_bin_mc1, ppb2_mc1, wry_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 41
first_imp %>% group_by(fi_bin_mc1, ppb2_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 14
first_imp %>% group_by(fi_bin_mc1, wry_bin_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 53
first_imp %>% group_by(ppb2_mc1, wry_bin_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 18

# poverty, food insec, household crowding
# poverty, food insec, deportation worry
# poverty, food insec, chronic stress
# poverty, household crowding, deportation worry
# poverty, household crowding, chronic stress
# poverty, deportation worry, chronic stress
# food insecurity, household crowding, deportation worry
# food insecurity, household crowding, chronic stress
# food insecurity, deportation worry, chronic stress
# household crowding, deportation worry, chronic stress


# 4 most common social stressors experienced together
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, fi_bin_mc1, ppb2_mc1, wry_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 28
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, fi_bin_mc1, ppb2_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 11
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, ppb2_mc1, wry_bin_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 11
first_imp %>% group_by(pov_binary, fi_bin_mc1, wry_bin_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 38
first_imp %>% group_by(fi_bin_mc1, ppb2_mc1, wry_bin_mc1, stress_bin_mc1) %>% count() # 12

# poverty, food insec, household crowding, deportation worry
# poverty, food insec, household crowding, chronic stressors
# poverty, household crowding, deportation worry, chronic stressors
# poverty, food insec, deportation worry, chronic stressors
# food insec, household crowding, deportation worry, chronic stressors


# Those who experienced all social stressors
first_imp %>% group_by(fi_bin_mc1, ppb2_mc1, wry_bin_mc1, stress_bin_mc1, pov_binary) %>% count() # 10




# Average number of participants (over all imputations) who experienced no social stressors
common_no_vars <- c("stress_bin_mc1", "wry_bin_mc1", "fi_bin_mc1", "pov_binary", "ppb2_mc1")

counts <- numeric(imp$m)

for (i in 1:imp$m) {
  comp_dat <- complete(imp, i)
  
  counts[i] <- sum(rowSums(comp_dat[common_no_vars] == 0, na.rm = TRUE) == 0)
}

mean_count <- mean(counts)


# Average number of participants (over all imputations) who experienced most common social stressor
rbind(mi.n.p(imp, "wry_bin_mc1"))


# Average number of participants (over all imputations) who experienced most common pair of social stressors
rbind(mi.n.p.by(imp, "fi_bin_mc1", BY = "wry_bin_mc1"))


# Average number of participants (over all imputations) who experienced 3 most common social stressors
common_3_vars <- c("pov_binary", "wry_bin_mc1", "fi_bin_mc1")

counts <- numeric(imp$m)

for (i in 1:imp$m) {
  comp_dat <- complete(imp, i)
  
  counts[i] <- sum(rowSums(comp_dat[common_3_vars] == 1, na.rm = TRUE) == 3)
}

mean_count <- mean(counts)


# Average number of participants (over all imputations) who experienced 4 most common social stressors
common_4_vars <- c("stress_bin_mc1", "wry_bin_mc1", "fi_bin_mc1", "pov_binary")

counts <- numeric(imp$m)

for (i in 1:imp$m) {
  comp_dat <- complete(imp, i)
  
  counts[i] <- sum(rowSums(comp_dat[common_4_vars] == 1, na.rm = TRUE) == 4)
}

mean_count <- mean(counts)


# Average number of participants (over all imputations) who experience all social stressors
common_all_vars <- c("stress_bin_mc1", "wry_bin_mc1", "fi_bin_mc1", "pov_binary", "ppb2_mc1")

counts <- numeric(imp$m)

for (i in 1:imp$m) {
  comp_dat <- complete(imp, i)
  
  counts[i] <- sum(rowSums(comp_dat[common_all_vars] == 1, na.rm = TRUE) == 5)
}

mean_count <- mean(counts)

```


```{r, supplemental figure 1: plot correlation matrix}
# Get data from first imputation
cor_data <- complete(imp, 1)

# Remove outcomes from first imputed dataset used for correlation matrix
cor_data <- cor_data %>% select(-memory_mc1, -execfun_rc_mc1, -verbal_rc_mc1, -global_rc_mc1)
                                #-age_qx_mc1)
                                #-ageusa_18, -married, -educcat_mom, -work_mc1, -lang_exam_mc1)

# Make variables numeric
cor_data <- cor_data %>% mutate(ageusa_18 = ifelse(ageusa_18 == "0", 0,
                                            ifelse(ageusa_18 == "<18", 1, 2)),
                                married = ifelse(married == "not married", 0, 1),
                                educcat_mom = ifelse(educcat_mom == "<=6th grade", 0,
                                              ifelse(educcat_mom == "7-11th grade", 1, 2)),
                                work_mc1 = ifelse(work_mc1 == "Did not work since last visit", 0, 1),
                                lang_exam_mc1 = ifelse(lang_exam_mc1 == "English", 0, 1),
                                stress_bin_mc1 = as.numeric(stress_bin_mc1),
                                wry_bin_mc1 = as.numeric(wry_bin_mc1),
                                ppb2_mc1 = as.numeric(ppb2_mc1),
                                fi_bin_mc1 = as.numeric(fi_bin_mc1),
                                pov_binary = as.numeric(pov_binary))

# Change order of variables
cor_data <- cor_data %>% select(pov_binary, fi_bin_mc1, ppb2_mc1, wry_bin_mc1, stress_bin_mc1,
                                age_qx_mc1, ageusa_18, married, educcat_mom, work_mc1, lang_exam_mc1)


# Create correlation matrix
cor_mat <- cor(cor_data)

colnames(cor_mat) <- c("Low Household Income", "Food Insecurity", "Household Crowding", "Deportation Worry", "Job Stress",
                       "Age", "Nativity", "Marital Status", "Education Level", "Employment Status", "Language")

rownames(cor_mat) <- c("Low Household Income", "Food Insecurity", "Household Crowding", "Deportation Worry", "Job Stress",
                       "Age", "Nativity", "Marital Status", "Education Level", "Employment Status", "Language")
# Create correlogram
corrplot(cor_mat, tl.col = "black")
```


```{r, estimate individual exposure propensity scores}
# Calculate propensity scores for individuals in ith imputed dataset
# j = exposure
# prediction = probability of being exposed to jth stressor given covariate values

pscore <- function(i, j) {
df_t <- mice::complete(imp, i)
  x <- predict(with(df_t, glm(get(stressor_measures[j]) ~ age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom +
                              work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1, family = "binomial")), type = "response")
 return(x)
}

include <- list()

for (j in 1:length(stressor_measures)) {
  
# calculate propensity scores for each imputed dataset 
pscores_list <- lapply(1:30, function(x) pscore(x, j))
pscores <- do.call(cbind, pscores_list) 

# make matrix of exposure levels for each imputed dataset 
observed_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) mice::complete(imp, x) %>% dplyr::select(stressor_measures[j]))
observed_e <- do.call(cbind, observed_ls)

# find the minimum of propensity scores for people who were exposed
# and the maximum of propensity scores for people who were unexposed 
# to ensure there is adequate support 

pscores_support_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) cbind(min(pscores[,x][observed_e[,x] == 1]), max(pscores[,x][observed_e[,x]==0])))

pscores_support <- do.call(rbind, pscores_support_ls)

# identify whether propensity scores are within the area of support for each imputed dataset 
include_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) as.numeric(pscores[,x] >= pscores_support[x,1] & pscores[,x] <= pscores_support[x,2]))

include[[j]] <- do.call(cbind, include_ls)
}

names(include) <- stressor_measures
```


```{r, estimate association with cognitive outcome for individual exposures}
#### Memory composite ####

# Conduct g-computation on each imputed dataset
gcomp_mi <- function(m, exposure) {

df_p <- mice::complete(imp, m)

# Include those who fell within the area of support for each imputed data set 
df_p <- df_p %>% filter(include[[exposure]][,m]==1)

df_p$exposure <- df_p[[exposure]]
fit <- glm(memory_mc1 ~ exposure + age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1, data = df_p)
nobs <- nobs(fit)

# Calculate the ATE via just the beta coefficient -- this works because it's a linear model 
exposure_name <- grep("^exposure", names(coef(fit)), value = TRUE)
ate <- coef(fit)[exposure_name] 

cov <- vcov(fit)
cov <- cov[exposure_name, exposure_name]

results <- data.frame(cbind(ate = ate, var = cov, N = nobs)) 
return(results)
}


# Combine results across imputations using Rubin's combining rules
all_results_mem <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 5, nrow = length(exposures)))

for (i in 1:length(exposures)) {

# variance calculations from https://amstat.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476908?needAccess=true
# var = average of complete data variances + variance of complete data means 
ate_M_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) gcomp_mi(x, exposures[i]))
ate_M_df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, ate_M_ls))
names(ate_M_df) <- c("ate", "var", "N")

results <- data.frame(cbind(rd = mean(ate_M_df$ate), var = mean(ate_M_df$var) + ((30+1)/30)*var(ate_M_df$ate)))
results$lb <- results$rd - 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$ub <- results$rd + 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$N <- mean(ate_M_df$N)

all_results_mem[i,] <- results 
}

rownames(all_results_mem) <- exposures
colnames(all_results_mem) <-  c("rd","var","lb","ub","N")
exp_col_name <- c("Chronic Stressors", "Deportation Worry", "Household Crowding", "Food Insecurity", "Low Household Income")
all_results_mem <- cbind(all_results_mem, exp_col_name)

all_results_mem <- as.data.frame(all_results_mem)

all_results_mem <- all_results_mem %>% mutate(Outcome = "Memory Composite",
                                              Type = "Individual")




#### Executive function ####

# Conduct g-computation on each imputed dataset
gcomp_mi <- function(m, exposure) {

df_p <- mice::complete(imp, m)

# Include those who fell within the area of support for each imputed data set 
df_p <- df_p %>% filter(include[[exposure]][,m]==1)

df_p$exposure <- df_p[[exposure]]
fit <- glm(execfun_rc_mc1 ~ exposure + age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1, data = df_p)
nobs <- nobs(fit)

# Calculate the ATE via just the beta coefficient -- this works because it's a linear model 
exposure_name <- grep("^exposure", names(coef(fit)), value = TRUE)
ate <- coef(fit)[exposure_name] 

cov <- vcov(fit)
cov <- cov[exposure_name, exposure_name]

results <- data.frame(cbind(ate = ate, var = cov, N = nobs)) 
return(results)
}


# Combine results across imputations using Rubin's combining rules
all_results_exec <- data.frame(matrix(ncol=5, nrow=length(exposures)))

for (i in 1:length(exposures)) {

# variance calculations from https://amstat.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476908?needAccess=true
# var = average of complete data variances + variance of complete data means 
ate_M_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) gcomp_mi(x, exposures[i]))
ate_M_df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, ate_M_ls))
names(ate_M_df) <- c("ate", "var", "N")

results <- data.frame(cbind(rd = mean(ate_M_df$ate), var = mean(ate_M_df$var) + ((30+1)/30)*var(ate_M_df$ate)))
results$lb <- results$rd - 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$ub <- results$rd + 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$N <- mean(ate_M_df$N)

all_results_exec[i,] <- results 
}

rownames(all_results_exec) <- exposures
colnames(all_results_exec) <-  c("rd","var","lb","ub","N")
exp_col_name <- c("Chronic Stressors", "Deportation Worry", "Household Crowding", "Food Insecurity", "Low Household Income")
all_results_exec <- cbind(all_results_exec, exp_col_name)

all_results_exec <- as.data.frame(all_results_exec)

all_results_exec <- all_results_exec %>% mutate(Outcome = "Executive Function",
                                                Type = "Individual")




#### Verbal fluency ####

# Conduct g-computation on each imputed dataset
gcomp_mi <- function(m, exposure) {

df_p <- mice::complete(imp, m)

# Include those who fell within the area of support for each imputed data set 
df_p <- df_p %>% filter(include[[exposure]][,m]==1)

df_p$exposure <- df_p[[exposure]]
fit <- glm(verbal_rc_mc1 ~ exposure + age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1, data = df_p)
nobs <- nobs(fit)

# Calculate the ATE via just the beta coefficient -- this works because it's a linear model 
exposure_name <- grep("^exposure", names(coef(fit)), value = TRUE)
ate <- coef(fit)[exposure_name] 

cov <- vcov(fit)
cov <- cov[exposure_name, exposure_name]

results <- data.frame(cbind(ate = ate, var = cov, N = nobs)) 
return(results)
}


# Combine results across imputations using Rubin's combining rules
all_results_verbal <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 5, nrow = length(exposures)))

for (i in 1:length(exposures)) {

# variance calculations from https://amstat.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476908?needAccess=true
# var = average of complete data variances + variance of complete data means 
ate_M_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) gcomp_mi(x, exposures[i]))
ate_M_df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, ate_M_ls))
names(ate_M_df) <- c("ate", "var", "N")

results <- data.frame(cbind(rd = mean(ate_M_df$ate), var = mean(ate_M_df$var) + ((30+1)/30)*var(ate_M_df$ate)))
results$lb <- results$rd - 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$ub <- results$rd + 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$N <- mean(ate_M_df$N)

all_results_verbal[i,] <- results 
}

rownames(all_results_verbal) <- exposures
colnames(all_results_verbal) <-  c("rd","var","lb","ub","N")
exp_col_name <- c("Chronic Stressors", "Deportation Worry", "Household Crowding", "Food Insecurity", "Low Household Income")
all_results_verbal <- cbind(all_results_verbal, exp_col_name)

all_results_verbal <- as.data.frame(all_results_verbal)

all_results_verbal <- all_results_verbal %>% mutate(Outcome = "Verbal Fluency",
                                                    Type = "Individual")




#### Global function ####

# Conduct g-computation on each imputed dataset
gcomp_mi <- function(m, exposure) {

df_p <- mice::complete(imp, m)

# Include those who fell within the area of support for each imputed data set 
df_p <- df_p %>% filter(include[[exposure]][,m]==1)

df_p$exposure <- df_p[[exposure]]
fit <- glm(global_rc_mc1 ~ exposure + age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1, data = df_p)
nobs <- nobs(fit)

# Calculate the ATE via just the beta coefficient -- this works because it's a linear model 
exposure_name <- grep("^exposure", names(coef(fit)), value = TRUE)
ate <- coef(fit)[exposure_name] 

cov <- vcov(fit)
cov <- cov[exposure_name, exposure_name]

results <- data.frame(cbind(ate = ate, var = cov, N = nobs)) 
return(results)
}


# Combine results across imputations using Rubin's combining rules
all_results_global <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 5, nrow = length(exposures)))

for (i in 1:length(exposures)) {

# variance calculations from https://amstat.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476908?needAccess=true
# var = average of complete data variances + variance of complete data means 
ate_M_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) gcomp_mi(x, exposures[i]))
ate_M_df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, ate_M_ls))
names(ate_M_df) <- c("ate", "var", "N")

results <- data.frame(cbind(rd = mean(ate_M_df$ate), var = mean(ate_M_df$var) + ((30+1)/30)*var(ate_M_df$ate)))
results$lb <- results$rd - 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$ub <- results$rd + 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$N <- mean(ate_M_df$N)

all_results_global[i,] <- results 
}

rownames(all_results_global) <- exposures
colnames(all_results_global) <-  c("rd","var","lb","ub","N")
exp_col_name <- c("Chronic Stressors", "Deportation Worry", "Household Crowding", "Food Insecurity", "Low Household Income")
all_results_global <- cbind(all_results_global, exp_col_name)

all_results_global <- as.data.frame(all_results_global)

all_results_global <- all_results_global %>% mutate(Outcome = "Global Function",
                                                    Type = "Individual")

# Combine all individual results into a single dataframe
tot_results <- rbind(all_results_mem, all_results_exec, all_results_verbal, all_results_global)
tot_results
```


```{r, supplemental figure 2: illustration of common support with propensity scores from first imputation for low household income}
imp_1 <- mice::complete(imp, 1)  # Get first imputed dataset

# Choose low household income as exposure
exposure_var <- stressor_measures[5]

# Fit the propensity score model
pscore1 <- predict(glm(
  formula = as.formula(paste0(exposure_var, " ~ age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1")),
  data = imp_1,
  family = "binomial"
), type = "response")


# Observed exposure values
exposure_val <- imp_1[[exposure_var]]

# Find area of common support
min_exp <- min(pscores[exposure_val == 1], na.rm = TRUE)
max_unexp <- max(pscores[exposure_val == 0], na.rm = TRUE)

pscore_support <- c(min_exp, max_unexp)


# Plot density curve
ggplot(data = imp_1, aes(x = pscore1, fill = factor(exposure_val))) + 
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "red", "1" = "blue"),
                    labels = c("Unexposed", "Exposed")) +
  labs(x = "Estimated propensity score", y = "Density", fill = "") + 
  geom_vline(xintercept = min_exp, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = max_unexp, linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r, estimate mixture propensity scores - propensity scores for each exposure while controlling for the others}
# Calculate propensity scores for individuals in ith imputed dataset
# j = exposure
# prediction = probability of being exposed to jth stressor given covariate values and other exposure values

pscore_mix <- function(i, j) {
  df_t <- mice::complete(imp, i)
  exposure <- exposures[j]
  
  coexposures <- exposures[-(match(exposure, exposures))]
  
  # regress exposure of interest on other exposures plus covariates 
  formula <- paste(exposure, "~",   paste(coexposures, collapse = "+ "), "+ age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1")
  
  
  x <- predict(glm(formula = formula, data = df_t, family = "binomial"), type = "response")
  return(x)
}


include_mix <- list()

for (j in 1:length(exposures)) {
  
  # calculate propensity scores for each imputed data set 
  pscores_list <- lapply(1:30, function(x) pscore_mix(x, j))
  pscores_mix <- do.call(cbind, pscores_list) 
  
  # make matrix of exposure levels for each imputed data set 
  observed_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) mice::complete(imp, x) %>% select(stressor_measures[j]))
  observed_e <- do.call(cbind, observed_ls)
  
  # find the minimum of propensity scores for people who were exposed
  # and the maximum of propensity scores for people who were unexposed 
  # to ensure there is adequate support 
  
  pscores_support_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) cbind(min(pscores_mix[,x][observed_e[,x]==1]), max(pscores_mix[,x][observed_e[,x]==0])))
  pscores_support_mix <- do.call(rbind, pscores_support_ls)
  
  # identify whether propensity scores are within the area of support for each imputed data set 
  include_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) as.numeric(pscores_mix[,x] >= pscores_support_mix[x,1] & pscores_mix[,x]<= pscores_support_mix[x,2]))
  
  include_mix[[j]] <- do.call(cbind, include_ls)
}

names(include_mix) <- stressor_measures

```


```{r, estimate G-computation for cognitive outcome for individual exposures while controlling for other exposures}

#### Memory composite ####
gcomp_mi_mix <- function(m, exposure) {
  
  df_p <- mice::complete(imp, m)
  
  df_p <- df_p %>% filter(include_mix[[exposure]][,m]==1)
  
  df_p$exposure <- df_p[[exposure]]

  coexposures <- exposures[-(match(exposure, exposures))]
  
  
  formula <- paste("memory_mc1 ~ exposure +", paste(coexposures, collapse = "+ "), "+ age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1")
   
  fit <- glm(formula, data = df_p)
  nobs <- nobs(fit)
  
  # calculate the ATE via the beta coefficient -- this works because it's a linear model 
  exposure_name <- grep("^exposure", names(coef(fit)), value = TRUE)
  
  ate <- coef(fit)[exposure_name] 
  
  cov <- vcov(fit)
  
  cov <- cov[exposure_name, exposure_name]
  
  results <- data.frame(cbind(ate = ate, var = cov, N = nobs)) 
  return(results)
}


# Combine results across imputations using Rubin's combining rules
all_results_mix_mem <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 5, nrow = length(exposures)))

for (i in 1:length(exposures)) {
# variance calculations from https://amstat.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476908?needAccess=true
# var = average of complete data variances + variance of complete data means 
ate_M_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) gcomp_mi_mix(x, exposures[i]))
ate_M_df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, ate_M_ls))
names(ate_M_df) <- c("ate", "var", "N")

results <- data.frame(cbind(rd = mean(ate_M_df$ate), var = mean(ate_M_df$var) + ((30+1)/30)*var(ate_M_df$ate)))
results$lb <- results$rd - 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$ub <- results$rd + 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$N <- mean(ate_M_df$N)

all_results_mix_mem[i, ] <- results
}

rownames(all_results_mix_mem) <- exposures 
colnames(all_results_mix_mem) <- c("rd","var","lb","ub","N")

exp_col_name <- c("Chronic Stressors", "Deportation Worry", "Household Crowding", "Food Insecurity", "Low Household Income")
all_results_mix_mem <- cbind(all_results_mix_mem, exp_col_name)

all_results_mix_mem <- as.data.frame(all_results_mix_mem)

all_results_mix_mem <- all_results_mix_mem %>% mutate(Outcome = "Memory Composite",
                                                      Type = "Mutually Adjusted")




#### Executive function ####
gcomp_mi_mix <- function(m, exposure) {
  
  df_p <- mice::complete(imp, m)
  
  df_p <- df_p %>% filter(include_mix[[exposure]][,m]==1)
  
  df_p$exposure <- df_p[[exposure]]

  coexposures <- exposures[-(match(exposure, exposures))]
  
  
  formula <- paste("execfun_rc_mc1 ~ exposure +", paste(coexposures, collapse = "+ "), "+ age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1")
   
  fit <- glm(formula, data = df_p)
  nobs <- nobs(fit)
  
  # calculate the ATE via the beta coefficient -- this works because it's a linear model 
  exposure_name <- grep("^exposure", names(coef(fit)), value = TRUE)
  
  ate <- coef(fit)[exposure_name] 
  
  cov <- vcov(fit)
  
  cov <- cov[exposure_name, exposure_name]
  
  results <- data.frame(cbind(ate = ate, var = cov, N = nobs)) 
  return(results)
}


# Combine results across imputations using Rubin's combining rules  
all_results_mix_exec <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 5, nrow = length(exposures)))

for (i in 1:length(exposures)) {
# variance calculations from https://amstat.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476908?needAccess=true
# var = average of complete data variances + variance of complete data means 
ate_M_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) gcomp_mi_mix(x, exposures[i]))
ate_M_df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, ate_M_ls))
names(ate_M_df) <- c("ate", "var", "N")

results <- data.frame(cbind(rd = mean(ate_M_df$ate), var = mean(ate_M_df$var) + ((30+1)/30)*var(ate_M_df$ate)))
results$lb <- results$rd - 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$ub <- results$rd + 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$N <- mean(ate_M_df$N)

all_results_mix_exec[i, ] <- results
}

rownames(all_results_mix_exec) <- exposures 
colnames(all_results_mix_exec) <- c("rd","var","lb","ub","N")

exp_col_name <- c("Chronic Stressors", "Deportation Worry", "Household Crowding", "Food Insecurity", "Low Household Income")
all_results_mix_exec <- cbind(all_results_mix_exec, exp_col_name)

all_results_mix_exec <- as.data.frame(all_results_mix_exec)

all_results_mix_exec <- all_results_mix_exec %>% mutate(Outcome = "Executive Function",
                                                        Type = "Mutually Adjusted")




#### Verbal fluency ####
gcomp_mi_mix <- function(m, exposure) {
  
  df_p <- mice::complete(imp, m)
  
  df_p <- df_p %>% filter(include_mix[[exposure]][,m]==1)
  
  df_p$exposure <- df_p[[exposure]]

  coexposures <- exposures[-(match(exposure, exposures))]
  
  
  formula <- paste("verbal_rc_mc1 ~ exposure +", paste(coexposures, collapse = "+ "), "+ age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1")
   
  fit <- glm(formula, data = df_p)
  nobs <- nobs(fit)
  
  # calculate the ATE via the beta coefficient -- this works because it's a linear model 
  exposure_name <- grep("^exposure", names(coef(fit)), value = TRUE)
  
  ate <- coef(fit)[exposure_name] 
  
  cov <- vcov(fit)
  
  cov <- cov[exposure_name, exposure_name]
  
  results <- data.frame(cbind(ate = ate, var = cov, N = nobs)) 
  return(results)
}


# Combine results across imputations using Rubin's combining rules  
all_results_mix_verbal <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 5, nrow = length(exposures)))

for (i in 1:length(exposures)) {
# variance calculations from https://amstat.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476908?needAccess=true
# var = average of complete data variances + variance of complete data means 
ate_M_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) gcomp_mi_mix(x, exposures[i]))
ate_M_df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, ate_M_ls))
names(ate_M_df) <- c("ate", "var", "N")

results <- data.frame(cbind(rd = mean(ate_M_df$ate), var = mean(ate_M_df$var) + ((30+1)/30)*var(ate_M_df$ate)))
results$lb <- results$rd - 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$ub <- results$rd + 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$N <- mean(ate_M_df$N)

all_results_mix_verbal[i, ] <- results
}

rownames(all_results_mix_verbal) <- exposures 
colnames(all_results_mix_verbal) <- c("rd","var","lb","ub","N")

exp_col_name <- c("Chronic Stressors", "Deportation Worry", "Household Crowding", "Food Insecurity", "Low Household Income")
all_results_mix_verbal <- cbind(all_results_mix_verbal, exp_col_name)

all_results_mix_verbal <- as.data.frame(all_results_mix_verbal)

all_results_mix_verbal <- all_results_mix_verbal %>% mutate(Outcome = "Verbal Fluency",
                                                            Type = "Mutually Adjusted")




#### Global function ####
gcomp_mi_mix <- function(m, exposure) {
  
  df_p <- mice::complete(imp, m)
  
  df_p <- df_p %>% filter(include_mix[[exposure]][,m]==1)
  
  df_p$exposure <- df_p[[exposure]]

  coexposures <- exposures[-(match(exposure, exposures))]
  
  
  formula <- paste("global_rc_mc1 ~ exposure +", paste(coexposures, collapse = "+ "), "+ age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1")
   
  fit <- glm(formula, data = df_p)
  nobs <- nobs(fit)
  
  # calculate the ATE via the beta coefficient -- this works because it's a linear model 
  exposure_name <- grep("^exposure", names(coef(fit)), value = TRUE)
  
  ate <- coef(fit)[exposure_name] 
  
  cov <- vcov(fit)
  
  cov <- cov[exposure_name, exposure_name]
  
  results <- data.frame(cbind(ate = ate, var = cov, N = nobs)) 
  return(results)
}


# Combine results across imputations using Rubin's combining rules
all_results_mix_global <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = 5, nrow = length(exposures)))

for (i in 1:length(exposures)) {
# variance calculations from https://amstat.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476908?needAccess=true
# var = average of complete data variances + variance of complete data means 
ate_M_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) gcomp_mi_mix(x, exposures[i]))
ate_M_df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, ate_M_ls))
names(ate_M_df) <- c("ate", "var", "N")

results <- data.frame(cbind(rd = mean(ate_M_df$ate), var = mean(ate_M_df$var) + ((30+1)/30)*var(ate_M_df$ate)))
results$lb <- results$rd - 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$ub <- results$rd + 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
results$N <- mean(ate_M_df$N)

all_results_mix_global[i, ] <- results
}

rownames(all_results_mix_global) <- exposures 
colnames(all_results_mix_global) <- c("rd","var","lb","ub","N")

exp_col_name <- c("Chronic Stressors", "Deportation Worry", "Household Crowding", "Food Insecurity", "Low Household Income")
all_results_mix_global <- cbind(all_results_mix_global, exp_col_name)

all_results_mix_global <- as.data.frame(all_results_mix_global)

all_results_mix_global <- all_results_mix_global %>% mutate(Outcome = "Global Function",
                                                            Type = "Mutually Adjusted")

# Combine all mutually adjusted results into one dataframe
tot_results_mix <- rbind(all_results_mix_mem, all_results_mix_exec, all_results_mix_verbal, all_results_mix_global)
```


```{r, figure of differences in cognitive outcomes for individually and mutually adjusted social stressors}
ind_mix_results <- rbind(tot_results, tot_results_mix)

ind_mix_results$exp_col_name <- as.factor(ind_mix_results$exp_col_name)
ind_mix_results$exp_col_name <- factor(ind_mix_results$exp_col_name, levels = c("Low Household Income", "Food Insecurity", "Household Crowding",
                                                                                "Deportation Worry", "Chronic Stressors"))

ggplot(data = ind_mix_results, aes(x = exp_col_name, y = rd, group = Type)) + 
  geom_point(aes(shape = Type), position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lb, ymax = ub), width=.2,
                 position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Outcome, levels = c("Memory Composite", "Executive Function", 
                                         "Verbal Fluency", "Global Function"))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 'dotted') +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Difference in Cognitive Outcome Z-Scores") +
```
