```{r, figure of differences in cognitive outcomes for individually and mutually adjusted social stressors}
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Low \n Household \n Income", "Food \n Insecurity", "Household \n Crowding", "Deportation \n Worry", "Chronic \n Stressors"))

ind_mix_results <- ind_mix_results %>% rename(Exposure = exp_col_name)
```


```{r, estimate G-computation for cognitive outcomes for joint effect, all exposures at once}
#### Memory composite ####
gcomp_mi_joint <- function(m, ej) {
  
  df_p <- mice::complete(imp, m)
  
  df_p <- df_p %>% filter(include_mix[[exposures_joint[[ej]][1]]][,m]==1 & include_mix[[exposures_joint[[ej]][2]]][,m]==1)
  
  coexposures <- exposures[-(match(exposures_joint[[ej]], exposures))]
  
  for (var in exposures_joint[[ej]]) {
  df_p[[var]] <- as.numeric(as.character(df_p[[var]]))
}
  form <- paste("memory_mc1 ~ ", paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse = "*"), " +", paste(coexposures, collapse=" + "), "+ age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1")
  
  fit <- glm(form, data = df_p)
  
  nobs <- nobs(fit)
  
  # calculate the ATE via sum of beta coefficients -- this works because it's a linear model 
  ate <- sum(coef(fit)[c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":"))])

  # calculate the SE via the delta method 
  cov <- vcov(fit)
  cov <- cov[c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":")),c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":"))]


  
  dm_var <- deltamethod(~ x1 + x2 + x3, 
              mean = coef(fit)[c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":"))], cov = cov, ses=T)^2

  
  results <- data.frame(cbind(ate = ate, var = dm_var, N = nobs)) 
  return(results)
}

# combine results across imputations using Rubin's combining rules
exposures_joint <- list(c("pov_binary", "fi_bin_mc1"),
                        c("pov_binary", "ppb2_mc1"),
                        c("pov_binary", "wry_bin_mc1"),
                        c("pov_binary", "stress_bin_mc1"),
                        c("fi_bin_mc1", "ppb2_mc1"),
                        c("fi_bin_mc1", "wry_bin_mc1"),
                        c("fi_bin_mc1", "stress_bin_mc1"),
                        c("ppb2_mc1", "wry_bin_mc1"),
                        c("ppb2_mc1", "stress_bin_mc1"),
                        c("wry_bin_mc1", "stress_bin_mc1"))


# variance calculations from https://amstat.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476908?needAccess=true
# var = average of complete data variances + variance of complete data means 

all_results_joint_ls_mem <- list()

for (ej in 1:length(exposures_joint)) {
  ate_M_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) gcomp_mi_joint(x, ej))
  ate_M_df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, ate_M_ls))
  names(ate_M_df) <- c("ate", "var", "N")
  
  results <- data.frame(cbind(rd = mean(ate_M_df$ate), var = mean(ate_M_df$var) + ((30+1)/30)*var(ate_M_df$ate)))
  results$lb <- results$rd - 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
  results$ub <- results$rd + 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
  
  results$N <- mean(ate_M_df$N)

  all_results_joint_ls_mem[[ej]] <- results 
}

all_results_joint_mem <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, all_results_joint_ls_mem)) 
rownames(all_results_joint_mem) <- unlist(lapply(1:length(exposures_joint), function(x) paste0(exposures_joint[[x]], collapse=":")))

pair_exp <- c("Low Household Income & Food Insecurity",
              "Low Household Income & Household Crowding",
              "Low Household Income & Deportation Worry",
              "Low Household Income & Chronic Stressors",
              "Food Insecurity & Household Crowding",
              "Food Insecurity & Deportation Worry",
              "Food Insecurity & Chronic Stressors",
              "Household Crowding & Deportation Worry",
              "Household Crowding & Chronic Stressors",
              "Deportation Worry & Chronic Stressors")

all_results_joint_mem <- all_results_joint_mem %>% mutate(Outcome = "Memory Composite",
                                                          Exposure = pair_exp)




#### Executive Function ####
gcomp_mi_joint <- function(m, ej) {
  
  df_p <- mice::complete(imp, m)
  
  df_p <- df_p %>% filter(include_mix[[exposures_joint[[ej]][1]]][,m]==1 & include_mix[[exposures_joint[[ej]][2]]][,m]==1)
  
  coexposures <- exposures[-(match(exposures_joint[[ej]], exposures))]
  
  for (var in exposures_joint[[ej]]) {
  df_p[[var]] <- as.numeric(as.character(df_p[[var]]))
}
  form <- paste("execfun_rc_mc1 ~ ", paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse = "*"), " +", paste(coexposures, collapse=" + "), "+ age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1")
  
  fit <- glm(form, data = df_p)
  
  nobs <- nobs(fit)
  
  # calculate the ATE via sum of beta coefficients -- this works because it's a linear model 
  ate <- sum(coef(fit)[c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":"))])

  # calculate the SE via the delta method 
  cov <- vcov(fit)
  cov <- cov[c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":")),c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":"))]


  
  dm_var <- deltamethod(~ x1 + x2 + x3, 
              mean = coef(fit)[c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":"))], cov = cov, ses=T)^2

  
  results <- data.frame(cbind(ate = ate, var = dm_var, N = nobs)) 
  return(results)
}

# combine results across imputations using Rubin's combining rules
# variance calculations from https://amstat.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476908?needAccess=true
# var = average of complete data variances + variance of complete data means 

all_results_joint_ls_exec <- list()

for (ej in 1:length(exposures_joint)) {
  ate_M_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) gcomp_mi_joint(x, ej))
  ate_M_df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, ate_M_ls))
  names(ate_M_df) <- c("ate", "var", "N")
  
  results <- data.frame(cbind(rd = mean(ate_M_df$ate), var = mean(ate_M_df$var) + ((30+1)/30)*var(ate_M_df$ate)))
  results$lb <- results$rd - 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
  results$ub <- results$rd + 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
  
  results$N <- mean(ate_M_df$N)

  all_results_joint_ls_exec[[ej]] <- results 
}

all_results_joint_exec <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, all_results_joint_ls_exec)) 
rownames(all_results_joint_exec) <- unlist(lapply(1:length(exposures_joint), function(x) paste0(exposures_joint[[x]], collapse=":")))

all_results_joint_exec <- all_results_joint_exec %>% mutate(Outcome = "Executive Function",
                                                            Exposure = pair_exp)




#### Verbal Fluency ####
gcomp_mi_joint <- function(m, ej) {
  
  df_p <- mice::complete(imp, m)
  
  df_p <- df_p %>% filter(include_mix[[exposures_joint[[ej]][1]]][,m]==1 & include_mix[[exposures_joint[[ej]][2]]][,m]==1)
  
  coexposures <- exposures[-(match(exposures_joint[[ej]], exposures))]
  
  for (var in exposures_joint[[ej]]) {
  df_p[[var]] <- as.numeric(as.character(df_p[[var]]))
}
  form <- paste("verbal_rc_mc1 ~ ", paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse = "*"), " +", paste(coexposures, collapse=" + "), "+ age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1")
  
  fit <- glm(form, data = df_p)
  
  nobs <- nobs(fit)
  
  # calculate the ATE via sum of beta coefficients -- this works because it's a linear model 
  ate <- sum(coef(fit)[c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":"))])

  # calculate the SE via the delta method 
  cov <- vcov(fit)
  cov <- cov[c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":")),c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":"))]


  
  dm_var <- deltamethod(~ x1 + x2 + x3, 
              mean = coef(fit)[c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":"))], cov = cov, ses=T)^2

  
  results <- data.frame(cbind(ate = ate, var = dm_var, N = nobs)) 
  return(results)
}

# combine results across imputations using Rubin's combining rules
# variance calculations from https://amstat.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476908?needAccess=true
# var = average of complete data variances + variance of complete data means 

all_results_joint_ls_verbal <- list()

for (ej in 1:length(exposures_joint)) {
  ate_M_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) gcomp_mi_joint(x, ej))
  ate_M_df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, ate_M_ls))
  names(ate_M_df) <- c("ate", "var", "N")
  
  results <- data.frame(cbind(rd = mean(ate_M_df$ate), var = mean(ate_M_df$var) + ((30+1)/30)*var(ate_M_df$ate)))
  results$lb <- results$rd - 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
  results$ub <- results$rd + 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
  
  results$N <- mean(ate_M_df$N)

  all_results_joint_ls_verbal[[ej]] <- results 
}

all_results_joint_verbal <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, all_results_joint_ls_verbal)) 
rownames(all_results_joint_verbal) <- unlist(lapply(1:length(exposures_joint), function(x) paste0(exposures_joint[[x]], collapse=":")))

all_results_joint_verbal <- all_results_joint_verbal %>% mutate(Outcome = "Verbal Fluency",
                                                                Exposure = pair_exp)




#### Global Function ####
gcomp_mi_joint <- function(m, ej) {
  
  df_p <- mice::complete(imp, m)
  
  df_p <- df_p %>% filter(include_mix[[exposures_joint[[ej]][1]]][,m]==1 & include_mix[[exposures_joint[[ej]][2]]][,m]==1)
  
  coexposures <- exposures[-(match(exposures_joint[[ej]], exposures))]
  
  for (var in exposures_joint[[ej]]) {
  df_p[[var]] <- as.numeric(as.character(df_p[[var]]))
}
  form <- paste("global_rc_mc1 ~ ", paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse = "*"), " +", paste(coexposures, collapse=" + "), "+ age_qx_mc1 + ageusa_18 + married + educcat_mom + work_mc1 + lang_exam_mc1")
  
  fit <- glm(form, data = df_p)
  
  nobs <- nobs(fit)
  
  # calculate the ATE via sum of beta coefficients -- this works because it's a linear model 
  ate <- sum(coef(fit)[c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":"))])

  # calculate the SE via the delta method 
  cov <- vcov(fit)
  cov <- cov[c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":")),c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":"))]


  
  dm_var <- deltamethod(~ x1 + x2 + x3, 
              mean = coef(fit)[c(exposures_joint[[ej]], paste0(exposures_joint[[ej]], collapse=":"))], cov = cov, ses=T)^2

  
  results <- data.frame(cbind(ate = ate, var = dm_var, N = nobs)) 
  return(results)
}

# combine results across imputations using Rubin's combining rules
# variance calculations from https://amstat.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476908?needAccess=true
# var = average of complete data variances + variance of complete data means 

all_results_joint_ls_global <- list()

for (ej in 1:length(exposures_joint)) {
  ate_M_ls <- lapply(1:30, function(x) gcomp_mi_joint(x, ej))
  ate_M_df <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, ate_M_ls))
  names(ate_M_df) <- c("ate", "var", "N")
  
  results <- data.frame(cbind(rd = mean(ate_M_df$ate), var = mean(ate_M_df$var) + ((30+1)/30)*var(ate_M_df$ate)))
  results$lb <- results$rd - 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
  results$ub <- results$rd + 1.96*sqrt(results$var)
  
  results$N <- mean(ate_M_df$N)

  all_results_joint_ls_global[[ej]] <- results 
}

all_results_joint_global <- data.frame(do.call(rbind, all_results_joint_ls_global)) 
rownames(all_results_joint_global) <- unlist(lapply(1:length(exposures_joint), function(x) paste0(exposures_joint[[x]], collapse=":")))

all_results_joint_global <- all_results_joint_global %>% mutate(Outcome = "Global Function",
                                                                Exposure = pair_exp)
```


```{r, plot pairwise results}
joint_results <- rbind(all_results_joint_mem, all_results_joint_exec, all_results_joint_verbal, all_results_joint_global)

#ind_mix_results$exp_col_name <- as.factor(ind_mix_results$exp_col_name)
#ind_mix_results$exp_col_name <- factor(ind_mix_results$exp_col_name, levels = c("Low Household Income", "Food Insecurity", "Household Crowding",
                                                                                #"Deportation Worry", "Chronic Stressors"))

ggplot(data = joint_results, aes(x = rd, y = Exposure)) + 
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = lb, xmax = ub), width=.2) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Outcome, levels = c("Memory Composite", "Executive Function", 
                                         "Verbal Fluency", "Global Function"))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 'dotted') +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  labs(x = "Difference in Cognitive Outcome Z-Scores", y = "")
```


```{r, supplemental figure 3: plot individual, mutually adjusted, and joint effects together}
# Clean up individual, mutually adjusted, and joint dataframes to combine into single dataframe
joint_results <- joint_results %>% mutate(Type = "Pairwise Adjustment")
joint_results <- joint_results %>% select(rd, var, lb, ub, N, Exposure, Outcome, Type)


#### Low Household Income & Food Insecurity ####
pov_fi <- ind_mix_results %>% filter(Exposure %in% c("Low Household Income", "Food Insecurity"))

pov_fi_joint <- joint_results %>% filter(Exposure == "Low Household Income & Food Insecurity")

pov_fi <- rbind(pov_fi, pov_fi_joint)


ggplot(data = pov_fi, aes(x = Exposure, y = rd, group = Type)) + 
  geom_point(aes(shape = Type, color = Type), position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lb, ymax = ub, color = Type), width=.2,
                 position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Outcome, levels = c("Memory Composite", "Executive Function", 
                                         "Verbal Fluency", "Global Function"))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 'dotted') +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Difference in Cognitive Outcome Z-Scores") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Low Household \n Income", "Food \n Insecurity", "Low Household Income & \n Food Insecurity")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "forestgreen", "blue3"))



#### Low Household Income & Household Crowding ####
pov_crowd <- ind_mix_results %>% filter(Exposure %in% c("Low Household Income", "Household Crowding"))

pov_crowd_joint <- joint_results %>% filter(Exposure == "Low Household Income & Household Crowding")

pov_crowd <- rbind(pov_crowd, pov_crowd_joint)


ggplot(data = pov_crowd, aes(x = Exposure, y = rd, group = Type)) + 
  geom_point(aes(shape = Type, color = Type), position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lb, ymax = ub, color = Type), width=.2,
                 position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Outcome, levels = c("Memory Composite", "Executive Function", 
                                         "Verbal Fluency", "Global Function"))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 'dotted') +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Difference in Cognitive Outcome Z-Scores") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Low Household \n Income", "Household \n Crowding", "Low Household Income & \n Household Crowding")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "forestgreen", "blue3"))



#### Low Household Income & Deportation Worry ####
pov_wry <- ind_mix_results %>% filter(Exposure %in% c("Low Household Income", "Deportation Worry"))

pov_wry_joint <- joint_results %>% filter(Exposure == "Low Household Income & Deportation Worry")

pov_wry <- rbind(pov_wry, pov_wry_joint)


ggplot(data = pov_wry, aes(x = Exposure, y = rd, group = Type)) + 
  geom_point(aes(shape = Type, color = Type), position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lb, ymax = ub, color = Type), width=.2,
                 position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Outcome, levels = c("Memory Composite", "Executive Function", 
                                         "Verbal Fluency", "Global Function"))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 'dotted') +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Difference in Cognitive Outcome Z-Scores") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Low Household \n Income", "Deportation \n Worry", "Low Household Income & \n Deportation Worry")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "forestgreen", "blue3"))



#### Low Household Income & Chronic Stressors ####
pov_chron <- ind_mix_results %>% filter(Exposure %in% c("Low Household Income", "Chronic Stressors"))

pov_chron_joint <- joint_results %>% filter(Exposure == "Low Household Income & Chronic Stressors")

pov_chron <- rbind(pov_chron, pov_chron_joint)


ggplot(data = pov_chron, aes(x = Exposure, y = rd, group = Type)) + 
  geom_point(aes(shape = Type, color = Type), position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lb, ymax = ub, color = Type), width=.2,
                 position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Outcome, levels = c("Memory Composite", "Executive Function", 
                                         "Verbal Fluency", "Global Function"))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 'dotted') +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Difference in Cognitive Outcome Z-Scores") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Low Household \n Income", "Chronic \n Stressors", "Low Household Income & \n Chronic Stressors")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "forestgreen", "blue3"))



#### Food Insecurity & Household Crowding ####
fi_crowd <- ind_mix_results %>% filter(Exposure %in% c("Food Insecurity", "Household Crowding"))

fi_crowd_joint <- joint_results %>% filter(Exposure == "Food Insecurity & Household Crowding")

fi_crowd <- rbind(fi_crowd, fi_crowd_joint)


ggplot(data = fi_crowd, aes(x = Exposure, y = rd, group = Type)) + 
  geom_point(aes(shape = Type, color = Type), position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lb, ymax = ub, color = Type), width=.2,
                 position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Outcome, levels = c("Memory Composite", "Executive Function", 
                                         "Verbal Fluency", "Global Function"))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 'dotted') +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Difference in Cognitive Outcome Z-Scores") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Food \n Insecurity", "Household \n Crowding", "Food Insecurity & \n Household Crowding")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "forestgreen", "blue3"))



#### Food Insecurity & Deportation Worry ####
fi_wry <- ind_mix_results %>% filter(Exposure %in% c("Food Insecurity", "Deportation Worry"))

fi_wry_joint <- joint_results %>% filter(Exposure == "Food Insecurity & Deportation Worry")

fi_wry <- rbind(fi_wry, fi_wry_joint)


ggplot(data = fi_wry, aes(x = Exposure, y = rd, group = Type)) + 
  geom_point(aes(shape = Type, color = Type), position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lb, ymax = ub, color = Type), width=.2,
                 position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Outcome, levels = c("Memory Composite", "Executive Function", 
                                         "Verbal Fluency", "Global Function"))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 'dotted') +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Difference in Cognitive Outcome Z-Scores") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Food \n Insecurity", "Deportation \n Worry", "Food Insecurity & \n Deportation Worry")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "forestgreen", "blue3"))



#### Food Insecurity & Chronic Stressors ####
fi_chron <- ind_mix_results %>% filter(Exposure %in% c("Food Insecurity", "Chronic Stressors"))

fi_chron_joint <- joint_results %>% filter(Exposure == "Food Insecurity & Chronic Stressors")

fi_chron <- rbind(fi_chron, fi_chron_joint)


ggplot(data = fi_chron, aes(x = Exposure, y = rd, group = Type)) + 
  geom_point(aes(shape = Type, color = Type), position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lb, ymax = ub, color = Type), width=.2,
                 position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Outcome, levels = c("Memory Composite", "Executive Function", 
                                         "Verbal Fluency", "Global Function"))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 'dotted') +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Difference in Cognitive Outcome Z-Scores") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Food \n Insecurity", "Chronic \n Stressors", "Food Insecurity & \n Chronic Stressors")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "forestgreen", "blue3"))



#### Household Crowding & Deportation Worry ####
crowd_wry <- ind_mix_results %>% filter(Exposure %in% c("Household Crowding", "Deportation Worry"))

crowd_wry_joint <- joint_results %>% filter(Exposure == "Household Crowding & Deportation Worry")

crowd_wry <- rbind(crowd_wry, crowd_wry_joint)


ggplot(data = crowd_wry, aes(x = Exposure, y = rd, group = Type)) + 
  geom_point(aes(shape = Type, color = Type), position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lb, ymax = ub, color = Type), width=.2,
                 position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Outcome, levels = c("Memory Composite", "Executive Function", 
                                         "Verbal Fluency", "Global Function"))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 'dotted') +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Difference in Cognitive Outcome Z-Scores") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Household \n Crowding", "Deportation \n Worry", "Household Crowding & \n Deportation Worry")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "forestgreen", "blue3"))



#### Household Crowding & Chronic Stressors ####
crowd_chron <- ind_mix_results %>% filter(Exposure %in% c("Household Crowding", "Chronic Stressors"))

crowd_chron_joint <- joint_results %>% filter(Exposure == "Household Crowding & Chronic Stressors")

crowd_chron <- rbind(crowd_chron, crowd_chron_joint)


ggplot(data = crowd_chron, aes(x = Exposure, y = rd, group = Type)) + 
  geom_point(aes(shape = Type, color = Type), position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lb, ymax = ub, color = Type), width=.2,
                 position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Outcome, levels = c("Memory Composite", "Executive Function", 
                                         "Verbal Fluency", "Global Function"))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 'dotted') +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Difference in Cognitive Outcome Z-Scores") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Household \n Crowding", "Chronic \n Stressors", "Household Crowding & \n Chronic Stressors")) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "forestgreen", "blue3"))



#### Deportation Worry & Chronic Stressors ####
wry_chron <- ind_mix_results %>% filter(Exposure %in% c("Deportation Worry", "Chronic Stressors"))

wry_chron_joint <- joint_results %>% filter(Exposure == "Deportation Worry & Chronic Stressors")

wry_chron <- rbind(wry_chron, wry_chron_joint)


ggplot(data = wry_chron, aes(x = Exposure, y = rd, group = Type)) + 
  geom_point(aes(shape = Type, color = Type), position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lb, ymax = ub, color = Type), width=.2,
                 position = position_dodge(.9)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Outcome, levels = c("Memory Composite", "Executive Function", 
                                         "Verbal Fluency", "Global Function"))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 'dotted') +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Difference in Cognitive Outcome Z-Scores") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Deportation \n Worry", "Chronic \n Stressors", "Deportation Worry & \n Chronic Stressors")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "forestgreen", "blue3"))

```


```{r, causal-analysis-using-lmtp}
# Make all exposures numeric for input into lmtp function
cham_propen$stress_bin_mc1 <- as.numeric(cham_propen$stress_bin_mc1)
cham_propen$pov_binary <- as.numeric(cham_propen$pov_binary)
cham_propen$wry_bin_mc1 <- as.numeric(cham_propen$wry_bin_mc1)
cham_propen$fi_bin_mc1 <- as.numeric(cham_propen$fi_bin_mc1)
cham_propen$ppb2_mc1 <- as.numeric(cham_propen$ppb2_mc1)

# create 30 imputations
imp <- mice(cham_propen, m = 30)

imputed_data <- lapply(1:30, function(i) mice::complete(imp, i))

# set up exposure list
A <- list("stress_bin_mc1", "wry_bin_mc1", "ppb2_mc1", "fi_bin_mc1", "pov_binary")

# set up confounder vector
confounders <- c("age_qx_mc1", "ageusa_18", "married", "educcat_mom", "work_mc1", "lang_exam_mc1")

# set up learners 
learners <- c("SL.mean", 
              "SL.glm", 
              "SL.gam", 
              "SL.rpart", 
              "SL.rpartPrune", 
              "SL.step.interaction")




#### MEMORY COMPOSITE OUTCOME ####
run_lmtp_dataset <- function(data, A, confounders, learners_trt, learners_outcome) {
  
  # Shift functions
  set_to_one <- function(a) {rep(1, length(a))}
  
  shift_factory <- function(exposure_name, shift_func) {
  function(data, A_names) {
    out <- vector("list", length(A_names))
    names(out) <- A_names
    
    for (a in A_names) {
      if (a == exposure_name) {
        out[[a]] <- shift_func(data[[a]])  # Apply shift only to one exposure at a time
      } else {
        out[[a]] <- data[[a]]              # Other exposures remain the same
      }
    }
    out
  }
  }
  
  shift_functions <- lapply(A, function(a) shift_factory(a, set_to_one))
  names(shift_functions) <- A
  
  plan(multisession, workers = length(A))
  
  results <- future_lapply(seq_along(A), function(i) {
  
    lmtp_tmle(data = data, 
              trt = A, 
              outcome = "memory_mc1", 
              baseline = confounders, 
              shift = shift_functions[[i]],
              mtp = TRUE,
              outcome_type = "continuous",
              learners_trt = learners_trt, 
              learners_outcome = learners_outcome, 
              folds = 1)
})

names(results) <- A
return(results)

}

all_results <- lapply(imputed_data, function(dat){
  run_lmtp_dataset(data = dat, 
                   A = A, 
                   confounders = confounders, 
                   learners_trt = learners, 
                   learners_outcome = learners)
})


# Extract estimates for executive function outcome
ests_by_exposure_mem <- estimates(all_results, A)



# Pool all results using Rubin's combing rules
pooled_results_mem <- lapply(ests_by_exposure_mem, function(df) {
  rubin_combine(df$estimate, df$se)
}) |> bind_rows(.id = "exposure")

pooled_results_mem <- pooled_results_mem %>% mutate(Outcome = "Memory Composite")




#### EXECUTIVE FUNCTION OUTCOME ####
run_lmtp_dataset <- function(data, A, confounders, learners_trt, learners_outcome) {
  
  # Shift functions
  set_to_one <- function(a) {rep(1, length(a))}
  
  shift_factory <- function(exposure_name, shift_func) {
  function(data, A_names) {
    out <- vector("list", length(A_names))
    names(out) <- A_names
    
    for (a in A_names) {
      if (a == exposure_name) {
        out[[a]] <- shift_func(data[[a]])  # Apply shift only to one exposure at a time
      } else {
        out[[a]] <- data[[a]]              # Other exposures remain the same
      }
    }
    out
  }
  }
  
  shift_functions <- lapply(A, function(a) shift_factory(a, set_to_one))
  names(shift_functions) <- A
  
  plan(multisession, workers = length(A))
  
  results <- future_lapply(seq_along(A), function(i) {
  
    lmtp_tmle(data = data, 
              trt = A, 
              outcome = "execfun_rc_mc1", 
              baseline = confounders, 
              shift = shift_functions[[i]],
              mtp = TRUE,
              outcome_type = "continuous",
              learners_trt = learners_trt, 
              learners_outcome = learners_outcome, 
              folds = 1)
})

names(results) <- A
return(results)

}

all_results <- lapply(imputed_data, function(dat){
  run_lmtp_dataset(data = dat, 
                   A = A, 
                   confounders = confounders, 
                   learners_trt = learners, 
                   learners_outcome = learners)
})


# Extract estimates for executive function outcome
ests_by_exposure_exec <- estimates(all_results, A)



# Pool all results using Rubin's combing rules
pooled_results_exec <- lapply(ests_by_exposure_exec, function(df) {
  rubin_combine(df$estimate, df$se)
}) |> bind_rows(.id = "exposure")

pooled_results_exec <- pooled_results_exec %>% mutate(Outcome = "Executive Function")




#### VERBAL FLUENCY OUTCOME ####
run_lmtp_dataset <- function(data, A, confounders, learners_trt, learners_outcome) {
  
  # Shift functions
  set_to_one <- function(a) {rep(1, length(a))}
  
  shift_factory <- function(exposure_name, shift_func) {
  function(data, A_names) {
    out <- vector("list", length(A_names))
    names(out) <- A_names
    
    for (a in A_names) {
      if (a == exposure_name) {
        out[[a]] <- shift_func(data[[a]])  # Apply shift only to one exposure at a time
      } else {
        out[[a]] <- data[[a]]              # Other exposures remain the same
      }
    }
    out
  }
  }
  
  shift_functions <- lapply(A, function(a) shift_factory(a, set_to_one))
  names(shift_functions) <- A
  
  plan(multisession, workers = length(A))
  
  results <- future_lapply(seq_along(A), function(i) {
  
    lmtp_tmle(data = data, 
              trt = A, 
              outcome = "verbal_rc_mc1", 
              baseline = confounders, 
              shift = shift_functions[[i]],
              mtp = TRUE,
              outcome_type = "continuous",
              learners_trt = learners_trt, 
              learners_outcome = learners_outcome, 
              folds = 1)
})

names(results) <- A
return(results)

}

all_results <- lapply(imputed_data, function(dat){
  run_lmtp_dataset(data = dat, 
                   A = A, 
                   confounders = confounders, 
                   learners_trt = learners, 
                   learners_outcome = learners)
})


# Extract estimates for executive function outcome
ests_by_exposure_verbal <- estimates(all_results, A)



# Pool all results using Rubin's combing rules
pooled_results_verbal <- lapply(ests_by_exposure_verbal, function(df) {
  rubin_combine(df$estimate, df$se)
}) |> bind_rows(.id = "exposure")

pooled_results_verbal <- pooled_results_verbal %>% mutate(Outcome = "Verbal Fluency")




#### GLOBAL FUNCTION OUTCOME ####
run_lmtp_dataset <- function(data, A, confounders, learners_trt, learners_outcome) {
  
  # Shift functions
  set_to_one <- function(a) {rep(1, length(a))}
  
  shift_factory <- function(exposure_name, shift_func) {
  function(data, A_names) {
    out <- vector("list", length(A_names))
    names(out) <- A_names
    
    for (a in A_names) {
      if (a == exposure_name) {
        out[[a]] <- shift_func(data[[a]])  # Apply shift only to one exposure at a time
      } else {
        out[[a]] <- data[[a]]              # Other exposures remain the same
      }
    }
    out
  }
  }
  
  shift_functions <- lapply(A, function(a) shift_factory(a, set_to_one))
  names(shift_functions) <- A
  
  plan(multisession, workers = length(A))
  
  results <- future_lapply(seq_along(A), function(i) {
  
    lmtp_tmle(data = data, 
              trt = A, 
              outcome = "global_rc_mc1", 
              baseline = confounders, 
              shift = shift_functions[[i]],
              mtp = TRUE,
              outcome_type = "continuous",
              learners_trt = learners_trt, 
              learners_outcome = learners_outcome, 
              folds = 1)
})

names(results) <- A
return(results)

}

all_results <- lapply(imputed_data, function(dat){
  run_lmtp_dataset(data = dat, 
                   A = A, 
                   confounders = confounders, 
                   learners_trt = learners, 
                   learners_outcome = learners)
})


# Extract estimates for executive function outcome
ests_by_exposure_global <- estimates(all_results, A)



# Pool all results using Rubin's combing rules
pooled_results_global <- lapply(ests_by_exposure_global, function(df) {
  rubin_combine(df$estimate, df$se)
}) |> bind_rows(.id = "exposure")

pooled_results_global <- pooled_results_global %>% mutate(Outcome = "Global Function")
```
